    14→import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from '@/components/ui/popover';
    15→import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation';
    16→import { FormService, FormWithFields, FieldType } from '@/lib/formService';
    17→import { CheckCircle2, FileText, Loader, Calendar as CalendarIcon, Upload, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react';
    18→
    19→// Create a standalone Supabase client for public access
    20→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    21→const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    22→const supabasePublic = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
    23→
    24→export default function PublicFormPage({ params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
    25→  const formId = params.id;
    26→  const searchParams = useSearchParams();
    27→  const clientId = searchParams.get('client');
    28→  const [form, setForm] = useState<FormWithFields | null>(null);
    29→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    30→  const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false);
    31→  const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState(false);
    32→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    33→
    34→  // Form data
    35→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<Record<string, any>>({});
    36→  const [validationErrors, setValidationErrors] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});
    37→
    38→  // Multiple answer tracking - stores arrays of values for fields that allow multiple
    39→  const [multipleAnswers, setMultipleAnswers] = useState<Record<string, string[]>>({});
    40→
    41→  // Other field tracking - stores custom text for "Other" option
    42→  const [otherFieldValues, setOtherFieldValues] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});
    43→
    44→  // Track which fields have "Other" selected
    45→  const [otherFieldSelected, setOtherFieldSelected] = useState<Record<string, boolean>>({});
    46→
    47→  // Track uploaded files for fields with attachments
    48→  const [uploadedFiles, setUploadedFiles] = useState<Record<string, File[]>>({});
    49→  const [uploadingFiles, setUploadingFiles] = useState<Record<string, boolean>>({});
    50→
    51→  // Track reason values for Yes/No selections
    52→  const [yesNoReasons, setYesNoReasons] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});
    53→
    54→  // Page navigation
    55→  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(1);
    56→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);
    57→
    58→  // Date formatting helpers
    59→  const formatDate = (dateString: string) => {
    60→    if (!dateString) return '';
    61→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString();
    62→  };
    63→
    64→  const formatDateForInput = (date: Date | undefined) => {
    65→    if (!date) return "";
    66→    const year = date.getFullYear();
    67→    const month = String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0");
    68→    const day = String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, "0");
    69→    return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
    70→  };
    71→
    72→  const parseDateFromInput = (dateString: string) => {
    73→    if (!dateString) return undefined;
    74→    const [year, month, day] = dateString.split("-");
    75→    return new Date(parseInt(year), parseInt(month) - 1, parseInt(day));
    76→  };
    77→
    78→  useEffect(() => {
    79→    fetchForm();
    80→  }, [formId]);
    81→
    82→  const fetchForm = async () => {
    83→    try {
    84→      setLoading(true);
    85→      const data = await FormService.getPublicForm(formId, supabasePublic);
    86→
    87→      if (!data) {
    88→        setError('Form not found or not published');
    89→        return;
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      if (data.status === 'closed') {
    93→        setError('This form is no longer accepting responses');
    94→        return;
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      setForm(data);
    98→
    99→      // Calculate total pages
   100→      const maxPage = data.fields.reduce((max, field) => Math.max(max, field.page_number), 1);
   101→      setTotalPages(maxPage);
   102→
   103→      // Initialize form data with empty values
   104→      const initialData: Record<string, any> = {};
   105→      data.fields.forEach(field => {
   106→        if (field.field_type === 'checkbox') {
   107→          initialData[field.id] = [];
   108→        } else {
   109→          initialData[field.id] = '';
   110→        }
   111→      });
   112→      setFormData(initialData);
   113→    } catch (error) {
   114→      console.error('Error fetching form:', error);
   115→      setError('Failed to load form');
   116→    } finally {
   117→      setLoading(false);
   118→    }
   119→  };
   120→
   121→  const validateCurrentPage = (): boolean => {
   122→    const errors: Record<string, string> = {};
   123→
   124→    if (!form) return false;
   125→
   126→    // Validate only fields on current page (skip section headers and descriptions)
   127→    const fieldsOnCurrentPage = form.fields.filter(f => f.page_number === currentPage && !['section', 'description'].includes(f.field_type));
   128→    fieldsOnCurrentPage.forEach(field => {
   129→      if (field.required) {
   130→        const value = formData[field.id];
   131→        if (!value || (Array.isArray(value) && value.length === 0)) {
   132→          errors[field.id] = 'This field is required';
   133→        }
   134→      }
   135→
   136→      // Validate email fields
   137→      if (field.field_type === 'email' && formData[field.id]) {
   138→        const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
   139→        if (!emailRegex.test(formData[field.id])) {
   140→          errors[field.id] = 'Please enter a valid email address';
   141→        }
   142→      }
   143→    });
   144→
   145→    setValidationErrors(errors);
   146→    return Object.keys(errors).length === 0;
   147→  };
   148→
   149→  const handleNextPage = (e?: React.MouseEvent) => {
   150→    e?.preventDefault();
   151→    e?.stopPropagation();
   152→    if (validateCurrentPage()) {
   153→      setCurrentPage(prev => Math.min(prev + 1, totalPages));
   154→      setValidationErrors({});
   155→    }
   156→  };
   157→
   158→  const handlePreviousPage = (e?: React.MouseEvent) => {
   159→    e?.preventDefault();
   160→    e?.stopPropagation();
   161→    setCurrentPage(prev => Math.max(prev - 1, 1));
   162→    setValidationErrors({});
   163→  };
   164→
   165→  const validateForm = (): boolean => {
   166→    const errors: Record<string, string> = {};
   167→
   168→    if (!form) return false;
   169→
   170→    // Validate required fields (skip section headers and descriptions)
   171→    form.fields.filter(f => !['section', 'description'].includes(f.field_type)).forEach(field => {
   172→      if (field.required) {
   173→        const value = formData[field.id];
   174→        if (!value || (Array.isArray(value) && value.length === 0)) {
   175→          errors[field.id] = 'This field is required';
   176→        }
   177→      }
   178→
   179→      // Validate email fields
   180→      if (field.field_type === 'email' && formData[field.id]) {
   181→        const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
   182→        if (!emailRegex.test(formData[field.id])) {
   183→          errors[field.id] = 'Please enter a valid email address';
   184→        }
   185→      }
   186→
   187→      // Validate Yes/No reason fields for select fields
   188→      if (field.field_type === 'select' && formData[field.id]) {
   189→        const selectedValue = formData[field.id].toLowerCase();
   190→        if (field.require_yes_reason && selectedValue === 'yes') {
   191→          if (!yesNoReasons[field.id] || yesNoReasons[field.id].trim() === '') {
   192→            errors[field.id] = 'Please provide a reason for selecting "Yes"';
   193→          }
   194→        }
   195→        if (field.require_no_reason && selectedValue === 'no') {
   196→          if (!yesNoReasons[field.id] || yesNoReasons[field.id].trim() === '') {
   197→            errors[field.id] = 'Please provide a reason for selecting "No"';
   198→          }
   199→        }
   200→      }

              .from('form-attachments')

            if (error) {

              throw new Error(`Failed to upload ${file.name}`);

      await FormService.submitResponse(
        {
          form_id: form!.id,
          response_data: responseDataWithFiles,
          client_id: clientId || undefined,
        },
        supabasePublic
      );

      // If thank you page is enabled, navigate to last page instead of showing full-page success
      if (form?.enable_thank_you_page) {
        setCurrentPage(totalPages);
      }
      setSubmitted(true);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error submitting form:', error);
      setError('Failed to submit form. Please try again.');
    } finally {
      setSubmitting(false);
    }

250:COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contact_links IS 'Links contacts to opportunities, partners, or affiliates with role info.';
251:COMMENT ON TABLE crm_stage_history IS 'Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics (conversion rates, velocity).';

## Detailed Codebase Analysis Report

Based on my thorough search of the KOL Campaign Manager codebase, here are the detailed findings:

---

### 1. User Roles & Authentication System

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`

**User Roles:**
- `super_admin` - Full system access
- `admin` - Administrative access to most features
- `member` - Standard team member with limited access
- `guest` - Read-only/minimal access
- `client` - Client account type

**User Profile Type:**
```typescript
type UserProfile = {
  id: string
  email: string
  name: string
  role: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'
  is_active: boolean
  profile_photo_url?: string | null
  telegram_id?: string | null
  x_id?: string | null
  created_at?: string
  updated_at?: string
}
```

**Key Authentication Methods:**
- `UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile(userId, email, name)` - Handles OAuth users
- `UserService.isCurrentUserAdmin()` - Role check
- `UserService.isCurrentUserSuperAdmin()` - Super admin check
- `useAuth()` hook provides: `user`, `userProfile`, `session`, `loading`, `signOut()`, `refreshUserProfile()`

**Role-Based Access Examples:**
- Telegram Chats: Super admin only (line 358 in Sidebar.tsx)
- Forms/SOPs: Admin or super_admin only (lines 444, 491)
- Field Options: Available to all (line 518)

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/userService.ts`

---

### 2. Dashboard & Overview Pages

**Architecture:**
- Main layout at `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/layout.tsx` wraps everything with AuthProvider and ChangelogProvider
- Home redirect (`/app/page.tsx`): Routes authenticated users to `/campaigns`, unauthenticated to `/auth`

**Dashboard Pages:**
- **Campaigns Page:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/campaigns/page.tsx`
  - View modes: Card view or Table view with pagination (12 items/page)
  - Search, filter by status (Active/Planning/Paused/Completed)
  - Create, edit, archive campaigns
  - Shows content counts (total & posted)

- **Clients Page:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx`
  - Client list with engagement info
  - Contains tabs: Overview, Campaigns, Forms, Delivery Logs, Meeting Notes, etc.
  - Status filters for active/inactive clients
  - Integration with `ClientService.getClientsForUser()` for role-based access

- **Sales Pipeline:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`
  - Kanban board with drag-and-drop (using @dnd-kit)
  - Multiple pipeline stages (12 total stages)
  - Shows opportunities with bucket classification (A/B/C)
  - Activity tracking with detailed CRM activities

- **Other Dashboard Pages:**
  - `/crm/network` - Partner/affiliate network
  - `/crm/contacts` - Contact management
  - `/crm/meetings` - Meeting calendar
  - `/tasks` - Task management (Andy only for testing)
  - `/daily-standup` - Daily standup view

---

### 3. Sidebar/Navigation Structure

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx` (1669 lines)

**Layout:**
```
Header
├── Logo + Version Badge (clickable changelog)
└── User Menu Dropdown (Settings, Sign Out)

Sidebar (collapsible via localStorage)
├── Holo GPT (Chat)
├── PEOPLE SECTION
│  ├── Team
│  └── Clients
├── KOLs SECTION
│  ├── KOLs
│  ├── Lists

│  ├── Links

│  ├── Field Options
│  ├── Changelog (Super Admin only)
│  └── Archive
└── Collapse Button
```

**Features:**
- Collapse/expand stored in `localStorage` as `sidebarCollapsed`
- Active nav item highlighted with theme color `#3e8692`
- Icons from lucide-react library
- Role-based menu visibility (admin/super_admin checks)
- Changelog modal accessible from version badge
- User avatar with initials fallback

---

### 4. Telegram Bot Integration

**Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/telegram/page.tsx`

**Telegram Features:**

  - `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`
  - `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`

  - `telegram_messages` - Message history
  - `telegram_commands` - Custom bot commands
- **Webhook Support:** `/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts` for bot message handling
- **Admin Panel:** `/crm/telegram` page shows:
  - Chat list with message counts
  - Linked opportunities/KOLs
  - Message thread ID for topic-based messaging
  - Command management interface

**Key Integration Points:**
- Opportunities can be linked to Telegram chats via `gc` field (group chat ID)
- Message history stored for audit trail
- Send API supports custom thread IDs for topic-based groups

---

### 5. Notification/Toast System

**Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/hooks/use-toast.ts`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/ui/toast.tsx`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/ui/toaster.tsx`

**Toast Implementation:**
```typescript
const { toast } = useToast();

// Usage:
toast({
  title: "Success",
  description: "Action completed",
  variant: "default" // or "destructive"
})
```

**Toast Features:**
- Max 1 toast at a time (`TOAST_LIMIT = 1`)
- Auto-dismiss after 1,000,000ms (can be customized)
- Reducer-based state management
- Supports: title, description, action, variant
- Built on Radix UI toast primitives
- Toast ID generation for unique tracking
- Position: top-right on desktop, bottom on mobile

**Integration:**
- Toaster component in root layout (`/app/layout.tsx`, line 37)
- Used throughout app for form submissions, errors, confirmations

---

### 6. Cron Jobs & Scheduled Tasks

**Current Status:** No traditional cron jobs or scheduled tasks found in the codebase.

**API Routes (but no scheduling logic):**
- `/api/embeddings/route.ts` - On-demand embedding generation
- `/api/chat/agent/route.ts` - AI chat handler
- `/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts` - Webhook receiver (passive)
- `/api/version/route.ts` - Version info endpoint

**Potential for Implementation:**
- Could use Vercel Cron (declared in next.config.js with `/api/cron/...` routes)
- Could use external services like: Trigger.dev, Inngest, or AWS EventBridge
- Could use Supabase Database Functions with `pg_cron` extension

---

### 7. Client Data Structure & "Tasks Per Client"

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts`

**Client Type:**
```typescript
type Client = {
  id: string
  name: string
  email: string
  location?: string | null
  is_active: boolean

}

type ClientAccessMember = {

  client_id: string
}
```

// Admins: See all non-archived clients
// Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
```

**Client Page Features (from `/app/clients/page.tsx`):**

- Engagement metadata: type, scope, start date, milestones

### 9. Key Service Classes & Patterns

**Service Pattern Used:**
Each service is a class with static methods for CRUD operations:

```typescript
export class SomeService {
  static async getAll(): Promise<Type[]> { }
  static async getById(id: string): Promise<Type | null> { }
  static async create(data: CreateData): Promise<Type | null> { }
  static async update(id: string, data: UpdateData): Promise<boolean> { }
  static async delete(id: string): Promise<boolean> { }
}
```

- `UserService` - User management
- `ClientService` - Client management with role-based access
- `CampaignService` - Campaign CRUD
- `CampaignTemplateService` - Campaign template management

- `SalesPipelineService` - Sales opportunity tracking
- `KOLService` - Master KOL database
- `BookingService` - Meeting/booking calendar
- `FormService` - Form creation and submission handling
- `TelegramService` - Telegram notifications
- `ChatService` - Chat message history
- `OpenAIService` - AI/GPT integration
- `MessageTemplateService` - Template management

## Database Schema & Services - Detailed Findings

### 1. TASKS TABLE & SCHEMA

**Database Location:** The `tasks` and `daily_standups` tables are NOT in the Supabase migrations or database.types.ts file. They appear to be managed **directly in Supabase without an explicit migration** (likely created through the Supabase dashboard).

**Tasks Table Structure (inferred from `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`):**
- `id`: UUID (Primary Key)
- `task_name`: TEXT (required)
- `assigned_to`: UUID (FK to users.id, nullable)
- `assigned_to_name`: TEXT (denormalized user name)
- `due_date`: DATE (nullable)
- `latest_comment`: TEXT (nullable) - **Single comment field, NOT threaded**
- `frequency`: TEXT (one-time, daily, weekly, monthly, recurring)
- `task_type`: TEXT (Admin & Operations, Finance & Invoicing, General, Tech & Tools, Marketing & Sales, Client SOP, Client Delivery, Performance Review, Research & Analytics)
- `link`: TEXT (nullable) - External URL reference
- `description`: TEXT (nullable)
- `status`: TEXT (to_do, in_progress, paused, ready_for_feedback, complete)
- `created_by`: UUID (FK to users.id)
- `created_by_name`: TEXT (denormalized)
- `sort_order`: INTEGER (for manual reordering)
- `created_at`: TIMESTAMP
- `updated_at`: TIMESTAMP

**Daily Standups Table Structure** (from `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/daily-standup/page.tsx`):
- `id`: UUID
- `user_id`: UUID (FK to users.id)
- `user_name`: TEXT
- `completed_yesterday`: TEXT (Yes/No)
- `priorities`: TEXT
- `output_goal`: TEXT
- `blockers`: TEXT (nullable)
- `submission_date`: DATE
- `submitted_at`: TIMESTAMP
- `created_at`: TIMESTAMP

---

### 2. SERVICE FILES

**No dedicated task service exists.** Tasks and daily standups are managed directly through Supabase in the page components.

**Relevant Service Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` - CRM entity management (Opportunities, Partners, Affiliates, Contacts, Stage History)
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/chatService.ts` - Chat sessions and messages (simple messaging, not threaded)
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/formService.ts` - Form management with attachment support
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/userService.ts` - User/team management

---

### 3. SUPABASE SCHEMA & TYPES

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/database.types.ts` (1,108 lines)

**Key Tables Present:**
- `campaigns`, `campaign_kols`, `campaign_templates`, `campaign_updates`
- `campaign_reports`, `campaign_report_files`
- `chat_sessions`, `chat_messages`
- `crm_opportunities`, `crm_partners`, `crm_affiliates`, `crm_contacts`, `crm_contact_links`, `crm_stage_history` (from migration 022)
- `clients`, `client_access_members`
- `form_responses`, `forms`, `form_fields`, `field_options`
- `payments`, `users`, `partners`, etc.

**Note:** `tasks` and `daily_standups` tables are NOT in the generated types file, meaning they were created outside the migration system.

---

### 4. COMMENT SYSTEM PATTERNS

#### Current Implementation:
**Simple Single-Comment Model:**
- Tasks use a single `latest_comment` TEXT field (denormalized, no threading)
- Chat system has `chat_sessions` → `chat_messages` (flat list, no threading)
- No native threaded/nested comment support exists

#### CRM Stage History Pattern (Audit Trail):
Location: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql` (lines 189-205)

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_stage_history (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  object_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
  object_id UUID NOT NULL,
  from_stage TEXT,
  to_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
  changed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  notes TEXT
);
```

**Key Pattern:** Uses a separate history table to track changes with `notes` field for context.

---

### 5. FILE UPLOADS & ATTACHMENTS

#### Form Attachments System:
**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

**Storage Bucket:**
- Bucket ID: `form-attachments`
- Public: `true`
- File Size Limit: 10MB
- Allowed MIME types: images, PDFs, Office docs, CSV, text files

**RLS Policies:**
1. "Anyone can upload form attachments" - INSERT permission for public form submissions
2. "Anyone can read form attachments" - SELECT permission (public bucket)
3. "Form owners and admins can delete attachments" - DELETE permission with verification

**Implementation in Forms:**
- File reference stored in response data with key `${fieldId}_attachments`
- Files uploaded to `form-attachments/formId/fieldId/filename`
- Public URLs stored in response_data
- Location: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/forms/[id]/page.tsx` (lines ~224-250)

```typescript
// File upload code pattern:
const fileName = `${fieldId}/${file.name}`;
const { error } = await supabasePublic.storage
  .from('form-attachments')
  .upload(fileName, file);

const { data: urlData } = supabasePublic.storage
  .from('form-attachments')
  .getPublicUrl(fileName);
```

#### Campaign Report Files:
Alternative attachment system in `campaign_report_files` table with:
- `file_name`, `file_size`, `file_type`, `file_url`
- `uploaded_by` FK to users
- `is_public` boolean
- `display_order` for ordering

---

### 6. RECURRING LOGIC PATTERNS

**Tasks System:**
- Simple `frequency` field enum: one-time, daily, weekly, monthly, recurring
- No recurring instances/recurrence rules - just metadata
- Manual execution/renewal required
- No automatic task generation or scheduling

**Daily Standups:**
- Manual daily submission per user
- `submission_date` tracks which day submitted
- No automatic generation or scheduling

---

### 7. AUTOMATIONS

**Automated Workflows:**
- Migration file: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/004_create_automated_workflows.sql`
- Purpose: General automation framework (schema not detailed in reviewed files)

**AI/Agent Automations:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/aiService.ts` - AI execution
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/advancedAIService.ts`
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/agentTools.ts`

No direct task automation or scheduled execution found.

---

### 8. DAILY STANDUP SYSTEM PATTERNS

**Architecture:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/daily-standup/page.tsx` (533 lines)

**Key Features:**
- Per-user, per-day submissions
- Form with: completed_yesterday (Yes/No), priorities, output_goal, blockers
- Grouped by `submission_date` and `submitted_at`
- Team member filtering and search
- Edit/delete existing submissions
- Check if user already submitted today

**Pattern for Future Tasks System:**
- Uses React hooks for form state management
- Directly calls Supabase CRUD operations
- Uses `useAuth()` for authentication
- Toast notifications for feedback
- Dialog-based forms for editing

---

### 9. MIGRATION STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/` (31 files)

**Key Migrations Relevant to Features:**
- `004_create_automated_workflows.sql` - Automation framework
- `007_add_agent_execution_logs.sql` - Agent execution tracking
- `009_create_forms_system.sql` - Forms system
- `013_add_allow_attachments_to_form_fields.sql` - Form field attachment flag
- `018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql` - File storage + RLS
- `022_create_crm_tables.sql` - Complete CRM schema (252 lines)
- `023_crm_rls_policies.sql` - Row Level Security policies

---

### 10. KEY PATTERNS FOR IMPLEMENTATION

**Recommended Patterns for Task Comments/Attachments:**

1. **Comments (Threaded):**
   - Create `task_comments` table similar to CRM stage history pattern:
     ```sql
     id UUID, task_id UUID, user_id UUID, content TEXT, 
     parent_comment_id UUID, created_at, updated_at
     ```
   - Supports nested threads via parent_comment_id
   - Users can reply to specific comments

2. **Attachments:**
   - Follow form-attachments pattern: separate storage bucket `task-attachments`
   - Store file metadata in `task_comment_attachments` table
   - Path format: `tasks/{taskId}/{commentId}/{filename}`

3. **Automations:**
   - Use existing `automated_workflows` table
   - Reference CRM stage history for audit trail patterns
   - Webhook/scheduled job execution pattern established

4. **Recurring Tasks:**
   - Create `task_recurrence_rules` table with: frequency, end_date, pattern_data
   - Generate instances on schedule (daily, weekly, monthly)
   - Pattern: parent task → instances (like campaign templates)

---

|------|------|
| Tasks Page | `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx` |
| Daily Standup Page | `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/daily-standup/page.tsx` |

# ClickUp to HHP Task Management Migration -- Implementation Plan

## Current State Summary

**Tasks table** (in Supabase, NOT in migration files):
- 16 columns: `id`, `task_name`, `assigned_to`, `assigned_to_name`, `due_date`, `latest_comment`, `frequency`, `task_type`, `link`, `created_by`, `created_by_name`, `sort_order`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `description`, `status`
- Single RLS policy: "Authenticated users have full access to tasks" (permissive, all operations)
- Only index: `tasks_pkey` on `id`
- 4 rows currently in the table

**Tasks page** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`, 1375 lines):
- Monolithic `'use client'` component with all Supabase queries inline
- No service layer -- direct `supabase.from('tasks')` calls throughout
- Type definition is local (lines 46-63), not from `database.types.ts`

**Existing patterns to follow**:
- Service classes: static methods, import `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` (see `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts`, `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts`)
- Migrations: numbered SQL files in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/` (currently up to `025`)
- Storage: buckets exist for `form-attachments`, `crm-attachments`, `client-logos`, `campaign-report-files`
- Telegram: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` with static `sendMessage()`, API route at `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`
- UI: shadcn/ui components, brand color `#3e8692`, React Quill for rich text
- No `vercel.json` exists (no cron jobs configured yet)

## Milestone 1: Foundation -- Service Layer + Schema Additions

**Goal**: Extract all inline Supabase logic into a service class. Add `client_id`, `parent_task_id`, and `priority` columns to the tasks table. Add missing indexes. Do NOT change any UI behavior yet.

### DB Migration: `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`

```sql
-- Add new columns to tasks table
ALTER TABLE tasks
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parent_task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS recurring_config JSONB,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS template_id UUID;

-- Add indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_assigned_to ON tasks(assigned_to);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_client_id ON tasks(client_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_parent_task_id ON tasks(parent_task_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_frequency ON tasks(frequency);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_created_by ON tasks(created_by);

COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.client_id IS 'Links task to a client for client-scoped views';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.parent_task_id IS 'Self-referential FK for subtasks';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.priority IS 'low, medium, high, urgent';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.completed_at IS 'Timestamp when status changed to complete';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.recurring_config IS 'JSON config for recurring generation: {interval, day_of_week, day_of_month, next_due, end_date}';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.template_id IS 'FK to task_templates if created from a template';
```

### New File: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts`

Create `TaskService` as a static class following the `ClientService`/`CRMService` pattern. Extract every Supabase call from `page.tsx` into this service:

```typescript
export class TaskService {
  // --- Core CRUD ---
  static async getAllTasks(): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTaskById(id: string): Promise<Task | null>
  static async createTask(payload: CreateTaskPayload): Promise<Task>
  static async updateTask(id: string, updates: Partial<Task>): Promise<Task>
  static async deleteTask(id: string): Promise<void>

  // --- Queries ---
  static async getTasksForUser(userId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTasksForClient(clientId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getSubtasks(parentTaskId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getOverdueTasks(): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTasksDueSoon(days: number): Promise<Task[]>

  // --- Inline edit helpers ---
  static async updateField(id: string, field: string, value: any): Promise<void>
  static async updateSortOrder(id: string, sortOrder: number): Promise<void>
  static async reorderTasks(moves: {id: string, sort_order: number}[]): Promise<void>
}
```

Types should be defined at the top of the service file (exported), matching the existing `Task` type from `page.tsx` but extended with the new columns (`client_id`, `parent_task_id`, `priority`, `completed_at`, `recurring_config`, `template_id`).

### UI Changes: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`

- Replace all `supabase.from('tasks')` calls with `TaskService.*` calls
- Import the `Task` type from `@/lib/taskService` instead of defining it locally
- No visible UI changes -- everything should work exactly the same
- The `client_id` field in the form can be added but hidden/optional at this stage

### Sidebar Change: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx`

- No changes in this milestone. Tasks link stays as-is at line 401-412.

### Dependencies: None (first milestone)

### Estimated scope: ~3 files touched, 1 new file created

## Milestone 2: Comments, Attachments, Client Linking

**Goal**: Replace the single `latest_comment` text field with threaded comments. Add file attachments to tasks. Add client selector to task form.

### DB Migration: `027_task_comments_and_attachments.sql`

```sql
-- Task Comments (threaded)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_comments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  user_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  parent_comment_id UUID REFERENCES task_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_comments_task_id ON task_comments(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_comments_parent ON task_comments(parent_comment_id);

-- RLS for task_comments
ALTER TABLE task_comments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task comments"
  ON task_comments FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert task comments"
  ON task_comments FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own comments"
  ON task_comments FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own comments or admins"
  ON task_comments FOR DELETE USING (
    auth.uid() = user_id OR
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Task Attachments
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_attachments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_size INTEGER,
  mime_type TEXT,
  uploaded_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  uploaded_by_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_attachments_task_id ON task_attachments(task_id);

ALTER TABLE task_attachments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can upload task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = uploaded_by);
CREATE POLICY "Uploaders and admins can delete task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR DELETE USING (
    auth.uid() = uploaded_by OR
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
```

### DB Migration: `028_task_attachments_storage.sql`

Follow the pattern from `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`:

```sql
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
VALUES (
  'task-attachments', 'task-attachments', false, 10485760,
  ARRAY['image/jpeg','image/png','image/gif','image/webp',
        'application/pdf','application/msword',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
        'text/plain','text/csv']
) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- Authenticated users can upload
CREATE POLICY "Auth users can upload task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
  );

-- Authenticated users can read
CREATE POLICY "Auth users can read task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT USING (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
  );

-- Uploader or admin can delete
CREATE POLICY "Uploader or admin can delete task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE USING (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND (
      auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
      OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin','super_admin'))
    )
  );
```

static async deleteComment(commentId: string): Promise<void>
static async getCommentCount(taskId: string): Promise<number>

### DB Migration: `029_task_subtask_checklist.sql`

Subtasks use the `parent_task_id` column added in Milestone 1. Additionally, add a lightweight checklist table for quick checklists within a task (distinct from full subtasks):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_checklist_items (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  text TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_done BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  display_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_checklist_task_id ON task_checklist_items(task_id);

ALTER TABLE task_checklist_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users full access to checklists"
  ON task_checklist_items FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL) WITH CHECK (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
```

interface MyDashboardStats {
  assignedToMe: Task[];
  overdue: Task[];

  completedThisWeek: Task[];
  totalAssigned: number;
  totalOverdue: number;
}

  tasksByMember: { userId: string; name: string; total: number; overdue: number; completed: number }[];
  tasksByClient: { clientId: string; name: string; total: number; overdue: number }[];
  overallOverdue: number;
  overallCompleted: number;
  staleTasks: Task[];      // no update in 7+ days, not complete
}

interface ClientDashboardData {
  client: { id: string; name: string };
  tasks: Task[];
  deliverables: Task[];    // tasks with task_type = 'Client Delivery'
  overdueCount: number;
}

## Milestone 4: Recurring Task Engine + Automations

**Goal**: Make the `frequency` field actually generate new task instances. Add status-triggered automations.

### DB Migration: `030_task_automations.sql`

```sql
-- Task automations / rules
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_automations (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,

  -- Trigger
  trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'status_change', 'due_date_passed', 'task_created', 'stale'
  trigger_config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- e.g. {from_status: 'in_progress', to_status: 'complete'}
  -- e.g. {days_overdue: 1}
  -- e.g. {stale_days: 7}

  -- Action
  action_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'assign', 'change_status', 'send_notification', 'create_task'
  action_config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- e.g. {assign_to: 'user_id'}
  -- e.g. {notification_type: 'telegram', message_template: '...'}

  -- Scope
  applies_to_client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),  -- null = all tasks
  applies_to_task_type TEXT,  -- null = all types

  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE task_automations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage task automations"
  ON task_automations FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Automation execution log
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_automation_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  automation_id UUID REFERENCES task_automations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  action_taken TEXT NOT NULL,
  details JSONB,
  executed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_automation_logs_task ON task_automation_logs(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_automation_logs_automation ON task_automation_logs(automation_id);

ALTER TABLE task_automation_logs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can view automation logs"
  ON task_automation_logs FOR SELECT USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
CREATE POLICY "System can insert automation logs"
  ON task_automation_logs FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true);
```

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`

This is the recurring task and automation engine, triggered by Vercel Cron (or external scheduler):

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/cron  (called by cron, secured with CRON_SECRET header)
// 1. Find recurring tasks where next_due <= today and status = 'complete'
// 2. Clone them with new due_date, status = 'to_do', update recurring_config.next_due
// 3. Find overdue tasks (due_date < today, status != complete) -> execute automations
// 4. Find stale tasks (updated_at < now - 7 days, status not complete) -> execute automations
// 5. Log all actions to task_automation_logs
```

### New File: `vercel.json` (project root)

```json
{
  "crons": [
    { "path": "/api/tasks/cron", "schedule": "0 1 * * *" }
  ]
}
```

### Service additions to `taskService.ts`

```typescript
// --- Recurring ---
static async getRecurringTasksDue(): Promise<Task[]>
static async generateRecurringInstance(parentTask: Task): Promise<Task>
static async calculateNextDueDate(config: RecurringConfig, currentDue: string): Promise<string>

// --- Automations ---
static async getActiveAutomations(): Promise<TaskAutomation[]>
static async evaluateAutomations(task: Task, event: AutomationTriggerEvent): Promise<void>
static async logAutomationExecution(automationId: string, taskId: string, action: string, details: any): Promise<void>
```

### Modification to `taskService.ts` -- status change hook

When `updateTask` or `updateField` changes `status`:
- If new status is `complete`, set `completed_at = now()`
- If old status was `complete` and new is not, clear `completed_at`
- Call `evaluateAutomations(task, { type: 'status_change', from, to })`

### UI Changes

- **Modify task detail modal**: Add `recurring_config` editor in the form when frequency is not `one-time`:
  - Day of week selector (for weekly)
  - Day of month selector (for monthly)
  - End date (optional)
  - "Auto-generate next task when completed" toggle

- **New file: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/automations/page.tsx`**
  - Admin-only page to manage automation rules
  - List existing rules with toggle active/inactive
  - Create new rule form: trigger type, trigger config, action type, action config
  - View automation execution log

### Dependencies: Milestones 1-3

## Milestone 5: Telegram Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports

**Goal**: Send overdue task notifications via Telegram. Generate weekly summary reports.

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts`

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/alerts  (called by cron, daily at 9 AM)
// 1. Query overdue tasks (due_date < today, status not complete)
// 2. Group by assigned_to
// 3. For each user with overdue tasks, send TG message via TelegramService.sendMessage()
// 4. Format: "You have X overdue tasks: [list with task names and due dates]"
```

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts`

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/weekly-report  (called by cron, Fridays at 5 PM)
// 1. Query all incomplete tasks
// 2. Query tasks completed this week
// 3. Query stale tasks (no update in 7+ days)
// 4. Format summary: per-member breakdown, overdue counts, stale tasks
// 5. Send to Operations TG chat via /api/telegram/send
```

```json
{
  "crons": [
    { "path": "/api/tasks/cron", "schedule": "0 1 * * *" },
    { "path": "/api/tasks/alerts", "schedule": "0 9 * * *" },
    { "path": "/api/tasks/weekly-report", "schedule": "0 17 * * 5" }
  ]
}
```

static async getOverdueTasksByUser(): Promise<Map<string, Task[]>>
```

### 7-Day Stale Rule enforcement

In the daily cron (`/api/tasks/cron`), add stale detection:
- Tasks with `updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'` and `status NOT IN ('complete', 'paused')`
- Auto-change status to `paused` or flag with a `stale` badge
- Send TG notification to the assignee

### UI indicator on task rows

In `page.tsx` (or extracted table component), add a visual "stale" indicator:
- If `updated_at` is older than 7 days and status is not `complete`/`paused`, show a small orange "Stale" badge next to the task name

## Milestone 6: Templates + Form-to-Task Integration

**Goal**: Allow saving tasks as templates for reuse. Connect the existing forms system to auto-create tasks from form submissions.

### DB Migration: `031_task_templates.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_templates (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,

  -- Template fields (pre-filled values for new tasks)
  task_name_template TEXT NOT NULL,
  default_assigned_to UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  default_frequency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'one-time',
  default_task_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'General',
  default_priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  default_client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),
  default_description TEXT,
  due_date_offset_days INTEGER,  -- e.g. 7 = due 7 days from creation
  recurring_config JSONB,

  -- Checklist template
  checklist_items JSONB,  -- [{text: "...", display_order: 0}, ...]

  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE task_templates ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task templates"
  ON task_templates FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage task templates"
  ON task_templates FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Form-to-task mapping
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS form_task_mappings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  form_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES forms(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  template_id UUID REFERENCES task_templates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,

  -- If no template, direct config:
  task_name_pattern TEXT,  -- Can include {{field_label}} placeholders
  assigned_to UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),
  task_type TEXT DEFAULT 'General',

  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE form_task_mappings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage form task mappings"
  ON form_task_mappings FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
```

### Service additions to `taskService.ts`

```typescript
// --- Templates ---
static async getTemplates(): Promise<TaskTemplate[]>
static async getTemplate(id: string): Promise<TaskTemplate | null>
static async createTemplate(data: CreateTemplatePayload): Promise<TaskTemplate>
static async updateTemplate(id: string, data: Partial<TaskTemplate>): Promise<TaskTemplate>
static async deleteTemplate(id: string): Promise<void>
static async createTaskFromTemplate(templateId: string, overrides?: Partial<CreateTaskPayload>): Promise<Task>
static async saveTaskAsTemplate(taskId: string, templateName: string): Promise<TaskTemplate>

// --- Form integration ---
static async createTaskFromFormResponse(formId: string, responseData: Record<string, any>): Promise<Task | null>
```

### Modification to Form Submission API

In `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/forms/submit/route.ts`, add a hook after saving the form response:
- Check `form_task_mappings` for the submitted `form_id`
- If an active mapping exists, call `TaskService.createTaskFromFormResponse()`
- The task name can use template variables like `{{Company Name}}` replaced from `response_data`

### New UI Files

- **`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/templates/page.tsx`**
  - List all task templates
  - Create/edit template form
  - "Create task from template" button
  - "Save current task as template" action in task detail modal

- **Add to task detail modal**: "Save as Template" button in the footer

- **Add to form builder** (in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/forms/page.tsx`):
  - New "Task Creation" section in form settings
  - Toggle "Auto-create task on submission"
  - Map form fields to task fields
  - Select template or configure directly

### Dependencies: Milestones 1-2 (service layer, comments/attachments)

---

## Implementation Order Summary

| Milestone | Scope | New Tables | New Files | Modified Files |
|-----------|-------|------------|-----------|----------------|
| **M1: Foundation** | Service layer, schema additions | 0 new, alter `tasks` | `lib/taskService.ts` | `app/tasks/page.tsx`, migration `026` |
| **M2: Core Features** | Comments, attachments, client linking | `task_comments`, `task_attachments` | `components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx`, `TaskComments.tsx`, `TaskAttachments.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `app/tasks/page.tsx`, migrations `027`, `028` |
| **M3: Dashboards + Subtasks** | My/Admin/Client dashboards, checklists | `task_checklist_items` | `app/tasks/my-dashboard/page.tsx`, `app/tasks/admin/page.tsx`, `app/tasks/client/[clientId]/page.tsx`, `components/tasks/TaskChecklist.tsx`, `components/tasks/SubtaskList.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `components/Sidebar.tsx`, migration `029` |
| **M4: Recurring + Automations** | Auto-generation, status triggers | `task_automations`, `task_automation_logs` | `app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`, `app/tasks/automations/page.tsx`, `vercel.json` | `lib/taskService.ts`, task detail modal, migration `030` |
| **M5: TG Alerts + Reports** | Overdue alerts, weekly summary, stale rule | 0 new | `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts`, `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` | `vercel.json`, `app/tasks/page.tsx` (stale badge) |
| **M6: Templates + Forms** | Task templates, form-to-task | `task_templates`, `form_task_mappings` | `app/tasks/templates/page.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `app/api/forms/submit/route.ts`, task detail modal, migration `031` |

---

## Key Architectural Decisions

1. **Subtasks vs. Checklists**: Two distinct concepts. Subtasks are full `tasks` rows with `parent_task_id` (assignable, have due dates, statuses). Checklists are lightweight `task_checklist_items` (just text + done boolean). This mirrors how ClickUp and similar tools separate the two.

2. **`latest_comment` backward compatibility**: Keep the `latest_comment` column on the `tasks` table. When a new comment is added via `task_comments`, auto-update `latest_comment` to the most recent comment's text. This preserves the inline table preview without requiring a join on every task list fetch.

3. **Recurring generation via cron, not DB triggers**: Using a Vercel Cron API route rather than PostgreSQL triggers or pg_cron. This keeps logic in TypeScript where it is testable and debuggable, and avoids needing Supabase extensions.

4. **No breaking changes to existing RLS**: The current RLS policy is wide-open for authenticated users. New tables follow a slightly tighter model (comments/attachments have owner-based write policies). The main `tasks` table policy remains unchanged to avoid breaking existing functionality.

5. **`client_action_items` left separate**: The existing `client_action_items` table (80 rows, used in the clients page) serves a different purpose -- it is an action board with court/phase fields specific to client delivery. It should NOT be merged into the tasks system. However, the Client Dashboard in Milestone 3 can optionally show both `client_action_items` and `tasks` filtered by `client_id`.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx` - The 1375-line monolith that must be refactored into service calls and have its modal extracted. Every milestone touches this file.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts` - New file. The central service class that all task CRUD, comments, attachments, dashboards, recurring logic, and template operations flow through.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts` - Reference pattern for how to structure the TaskService (static class, Supabase queries, typed returns).
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql` - Reference pattern for the task-attachments storage bucket migration.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` - Used directly in Milestone 5 for sending overdue alerts and weekly reports.

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-17 09:34:25Z)**_

Design a detailed implementation plan for migrating from ClickUp to HHP's task management system. This is a large multi-phase project. The plan should be scoped to what needs to be BUILT in the codebase, organized into implementable milestones.

## CURRENT STATE

### Tasks Page (`app/tasks/page.tsx`, 1375 lines)
- Monolithic client component, no service layer
- Direct Supabase queries
- Fields: task_name, assigned_to, assigned_to_name, due_date, latest_comment (single text field), frequency, task_type, link, description (rich text), status, created_by, created_by_name, sort_order
- 5 statuses: to_do, in_progress, paused, ready_for_feedback, complete
- 3 tabs: One-Time, Recurring, Client SOP
- Grouped by assignee with collapsible sections
- Inline editing (double-click cells) + modal form
- Column reorder via drag-and-drop, persisted to localStorage
- No service file exists - all Supabase calls inline

### Existing Infrastructure
- Supabase backend (PostgreSQL) with RLS policies
- Auth: roles = super_admin, admin, member, guest, client
- File upload pattern exists in form-attachments bucket (10MB limit, Supabase storage)
- Telegram bot integration exists (`lib/telegramService.ts`) with sendMessage, webhook
- Toast notification system
- Service pattern: static class methods (UserService, ClientService, CRMService, etc.)
- Sidebar: `components/Sidebar.tsx` (1669 lines), role-based visibility
- Client data: clients table with client_access_members for role-based access
- No cron/scheduled tasks exist yet
- `sql/migrations/` directory has 31 migration files
- React Quill for rich text editing

### What the Migration Plan Requires (from the HTML spec doc)
1. **Threaded comments** (currently just a single `latest_comment` text field)
2. **File attachments** on tasks (currently only URL links)
3. **Recurring task auto-generation** (currently frequency is metadata-only)
4. **Subtasks/checklists** within tasks
5. **Automations** (status triggers, auto-assign, reminders)
6. **Form → Task creation** (forms system exists but not connected to tasks)
7. **My Dashboard** (per team member - assigned tasks, overdue, upcoming)
8. **Admin Dashboard** (oversight - tasks per member, per client, overdue counts)
9. **Client Dashboard** (per-client task view with deliverables)
10. **Client field on tasks** (tasks need to be linkable to clients)
11. **TG Bot alerts** for overdue tasks
12. **Weekly reports** (incomplete tasks, Friday summary)
13. **7-day stale rule** enforcement
14. **Task templates**

### Constraints
- Barebones stability first, optimization later
- Must not break existing task functionality
- Follow existing patterns (service classes, Supabase migrations, shadcn/ui components)
- Tasks table exists but NOT in migrations - need to be careful with schema changes

## DELIVERABLE

Create a phased implementation plan with concrete milestones. For each milestone:
- What DB migrations are needed
- What service files to create/modify
- What UI changes to make
- What API routes are needed (if any)
- Dependencies on previous milestones

Organize into 5-6 milestones ordered by priority:
1. Foundation (service layer, schema additions)
2. Core features (comments, attachments, client linking)
3. Dashboards
4. Recurring/automation engine
5. TG bot alerts & reports
6. Polish (templates, form→task)

Be specific about table schemas, file paths, and component structure. This needs to be actionable.

 `./lib/clientService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
     6→type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
     9→  member_count?: number;
    10→  campaign_count?: number;
    11→  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class ClientService {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Get clients based on user role
    17→   * - Admins: See all clients
    18→   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
    19→   */
    20→  static async getClientsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string): Promise<ClientWithAccess[]> {
    21→    try {
    22→      if (userRole === 'admin' || userRole === 'super_admin') {
    23→        // Admins and super admins can see all non-archived clients
    24→        const { data: clients, error } = await supabase
    25→          .from('clients')
    26→          .select(`
    27→            *,
    28→            client_access_members(count),
    29→            campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    30→            whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    31→          `)
    32→          .is('archived_at', null)
    33→          .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→        if (error) throw error;
    36→
    37→        return clients?.map(client => ({
    38→          ...client,
    39→          member_count: client.client_access_members?.[0]?.count || 0,
    40→          campaign_count: (client as any).campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    41→          whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    42→        })) || [];
    43→      } else {
    44→        // Members can only see non-archived clients they have access to
    45→        const { data: clientAccess, error } = await supabase
    46→          .from('client_access_members')
    47→          .select(`
    48→            clients!inner(
    49→              id,
    50→              name,
    51→              email,
    52→              location,
    53→              is_active,
    54→              archived_at,
    55→              created_at,
    56→              updated_at,
    57→              logo_url,
    58→              campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    59→              whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    60→            )
    61→          `)
    62→          .eq('user_id', userId);
    63→
    64→        if (error) throw error;
    65→
    66→        return clientAccess?.map(access => {
    67→          const client = (access as any).clients;
    68→          return {
    69→            ...client,
    70→            campaign_count: client.campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    71→            whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    72→          };
    73→        }).filter(c => c && !c.archived_at) || [];
    74→      }
    75→    } catch (error) {
    76→      console.error('Error fetching clients:', error);
    77→      throw error;
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
```

   103→
   104→const frequencyBadge = (freq: string) => {
   105→  switch (freq) {
   106→    case 'one-time': return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
   107→    case 'daily': return 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800';
   108→    case 'weekly': return 'bg-green-100 text-green-800';
   109→    case 'monthly': return 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800';
   110→    case 'recurring': return 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-800';
   111→    default: return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
   112→  }
   113→};
   114→
   115→const typeBadge = (type: string) => {
   116→  switch (type) {
   117→    case 'Admin & Operations': return 'bg-slate-100 text-slate-700';
   118→    case 'Finance & Invoicing': return 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800';
   119→    case 'General': return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
   120→    case 'Tech & Tools': return 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800';
   121→    case 'Marketing & Sales': return 'bg-pink-100 text-pink-800';
   122→    case 'Client SOP': return 'bg-amber-100 text-amber-800';
   123→    case 'Client Delivery': return 'bg-cyan-100 text-cyan-800';
   124→    case 'Performance Review': return 'bg-violet-100 text-violet-800';
   125→    case 'Research & Analytics': return 'bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-800';
   126→    default: return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
   127→  }
   128→};
   129→
   130→const getDueDateColor = (dueDate: string | null) => {
   131→  if (!dueDate) return '';
   132→  const today = new Date();
   133→  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
   134→  const due = new Date(dueDate + 'T00:00:00');
   135→  const diffDays = Math.ceil((due.getTime() - today.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   136→  if (diffDays < 0) return 'text-red-600 font-semibold';
   137→  if (diffDays <= 3) return 'text-amber-600 font-semibold';
   138→  return 'text-gray-600';
   139→};
   140→
   141→/** Format a Date to YYYY-MM-DD in local timezone (avoids UTC offset issues) */
   142→const toLocalDateString = (date: Date) => {
   143→  const year = date.getFullYear();
   144→  const month = String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
   145→  const day = String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
   146→  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
   147→};
   148→
   149→// Fixed column widths for consistency across all tables
   150→const COL: Record<string, string> = {
   151→  reorder: 'w-[50px]',
   152→  status: 'w-[36px]',

   163→
   164→type ColumnKey = 'taskName' | 'dueDate' | 'comment' | 'frequency' | 'type' | 'link' | 'createdBy' | 'created';
   165→
   166→const COLUMN_DEFS: { key: ColumnKey; label: string }[] = [
   167→  { key: 'taskName', label: 'Task Name' },
   168→  { key: 'dueDate', label: 'Due Date' },
   169→  { key: 'comment', label: 'Comment' },
   170→  { key: 'frequency', label: 'Frequency' },
   171→  { key: 'type', label: 'Type' },
   172→  { key: 'link', label: 'Link' },
   173→  { key: 'createdBy', label: 'Created By' },
   174→  { key: 'created', label: 'Created' },
   175→];
   176→
   177→const DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER: ColumnKey[] = COLUMN_DEFS.map(c => c.key);
   178→const COLUMN_ORDER_KEY = 'tasks-column-order';
   179→
   180→export default function TasksPage() {
   181→  const { user, userProfile } = useAuth();
   182→  const { toast } = useToast();
   183→
   184→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<Task[]>([]);

   187→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<string>('one-time');
   188→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
   189→  const [collapsedUsers, setCollapsedUsers] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
   190→
   191→  // Column order (persisted to localStorage)
   192→  const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<ColumnKey[]>(DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER);
   193→  const dragColRef = useRef<ColumnKey | null>(null);
   194→  const dragOverColRef = useRef<ColumnKey | null>(null);
   195→
   196→  useEffect(() => {
   197→    try {
   198→      const saved = localStorage.getItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY);
   199→      if (saved) {
   200→        const parsed = JSON.parse(saved) as ColumnKey[];
   201→        // Validate: must contain exactly the same keys
   202→        if (parsed.length === DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER.length && DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER.every(k => parsed.includes(k))) {
   203→          setColumnOrder(parsed);
   204→        }
   205→      }
   206→    } catch {}
   207→  }, []);
   208→
   209→  const handleColumnDragStart = (col: ColumnKey) => {
   210→    dragColRef.current = col;
   211→  };
   212→
   213→  const handleColumnDragOver = (e: React.DragEvent, col: ColumnKey) => {
   214→    e.preventDefault();
   215→    dragOverColRef.current = col;
   216→  };
   217→
   218→  const handleColumnDrop = () => {
   219→    const from = dragColRef.current;
   220→    const to = dragOverColRef.current;
   221→    dragColRef.current = null;
   222→    dragOverColRef.current = null;
   223→    if (!from || !to || from === to) return;
   224→
   225→    setColumnOrder(prev => {
   226→      const newOrder = [...prev];
   227→      const fromIdx = newOrder.indexOf(from);
   228→      const toIdx = newOrder.indexOf(to);
   229→      newOrder.splice(fromIdx, 1);
   230→      newOrder.splice(toIdx, 0, from);
   231→      localStorage.setItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY, JSON.stringify(newOrder));
   232→      return newOrder;
   233→    });
   234→  };
   235→

   237→  const [editingCell, setEditingCell] = useState<EditingCell>(null);
   238→  const [editingValue, setEditingValue] = useState<string>('');

   247→  const [deletingId, setDeletingId] = useState<string | null>(null);

     4→// TypeScript types for Forms
     5→export type FormStatus = 'draft' | 'published' | 'closed';
     6→export type FieldType = 'text' | 'textarea' | 'email' | 'number' | 'select' | 'radio' | 'checkbox' | 'date' | 'section' | 'description';
     7→
     8→export interface Form {
     9→  id: string;
    10→  user_id: string;
    11→  name: string;
    12→  slug: string | null;
    13→  description: string | null;
    14→  status: FormStatus;
    15→  subdomain_enabled: boolean | null;
    16→  subdomain_url: string | null;
    17→  created_at: string;
    18→  updated_at: string;
    19→}
    20→
    21→export interface FormField {
    22→  id: string;
    23→  form_id: string;
    24→  field_type: FieldType;
    25→  label: string;
    26→  required: boolean;

    28→  allow_multiple: boolean | null; // Allow multiple answers
    29→  include_other: boolean | null; // Include "Other" option for select fields
    30→  allow_attachments: boolean | null; // Allow file attachments for text/textarea fields

    32→  require_yes_reason: boolean | null; // Require reason input when "Yes" is selected
    33→  require_no_reason: boolean | null; // Require reason input when "No" is selected
    34→  display_order: number;
    35→  page_number: number;
    36→  created_at: string;
    37→}
    38→
    39→export interface FormResponse {

    45→  client_id: string | null;
    46→  client_name?: string | null;
    47→  submitted_at: string;
    48→}
    49→
    50→export interface FormWithFields extends Form {

    53→

    56→}

   296→    tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP').length,
   297→    [tasks]

   300→    tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP').length,

   304→  // Filter tasks by tab + search

   306→    let tabFiltered: Task[];

   308→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   309→    } else if (activeTab === 'recurring') {
   310→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   311→    } else {
   312→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP');
   313→    }
   314→
   315→    if (!searchTerm) return tabFiltered.sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);

   317→    const term = searchTerm.toLowerCase();

   320→      (t.assigned_to_name && t.assigned_to_name.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   321→      (t.latest_comment && t.latest_comment.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   322→      t.task_type.toLowerCase().includes(term) ||
   323→      (t.created_by_name && t.created_by_name.toLowerCase().includes(term))
   324→    ).sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   325→  }, [tasks, activeTab, searchTerm]);

   328→  const groupedByUser = useMemo(() => {
   329→    const groups: { key: string; label: string; tasks: Task[] }[] = [];
   330→    const map = new Map<string, Task[]>();
   331→
   332→    for (const task of filtered) {
   333→      const key = task.assigned_to || '_unassigned';
   334→      if (!map.has(key)) map.set(key, []);
   335→      map.get(key)!.push(task);
   336→    }
   337→
   338→    const sortedKeys = Array.from(map.keys()).sort((a, b) => {
   339→      if (a === '_unassigned') return 1;
   340→      if (b === '_unassigned') return -1;
   341→      const nameA = map.get(a)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   342→      const nameB = map.get(b)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   343→      return nameA.localeCompare(nameB);
   344→    });
   345→
   346→    for (const key of sortedKeys) {
   347→      const tasks = map.get(key)!;
   348→      const label = key === '_unassigned' ? 'Unassigned' : (tasks[0].assigned_to_name || 'Unknown');
```
</details></tool-use>

   384→        [field]: value,
   385→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),

   389→      await fetchTasks();
   390→    }
   391→  };

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql`

```text
     1→-- CRM Database Schema
     2→-- Core Tables: Opportunities (Leads+Deals), Partners, Affiliates, Contacts
     3→-- Supporting: Stage History for analytics
     4→
     5→-- ============================================
     6→-- 1. AFFILIATES TABLE (created first for FK reference)
     7→-- KOLs, referrers with rev-share or commission
     8→-- Lifecycle: New → Active → Inactive
     9→-- ============================================
    10→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_affiliates (
    11→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    12→
    13→  -- Basic Info
    14→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→  affiliation TEXT, -- Their affiliation/company
    16→  category TEXT, -- Type of affiliate
    17→
    18→  -- Lifecycle
    19→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new', -- 'new', 'active', 'inactive'
    20→
    21→  -- Commission
    22→  commission_model TEXT, -- Description of commission/rev-share structure
    23→  commission_rate DECIMAL, -- Percentage if applicable
    24→
    25→  -- Tracking
    26→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    27→
    28→  -- Ownership
    29→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    30→
    31→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
    32→  poc_name TEXT,
    33→  poc_email TEXT,
    34→  poc_telegram TEXT,
    35→
    36→  -- Notes
    37→  notes TEXT,
    38→
    39→  -- Timestamps
    40→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    41→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
    42→);
    43→
    44→-- ============================================
    45→-- 2. OPPORTUNITIES TABLE (Unified Leads + Deals)
    46→-- Single pipeline: New → Contacted → Qualified → Proposal → Contract → Closed
    47→-- ============================================
    48→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_opportunities (
    49→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    50→
    51→  -- Basic Info
    52→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    53→
    54→  -- Pipeline Stage (unified lead + deal stages)
    55→  stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new',
    56→  -- Lead stages: 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'
    57→  -- Deal stages: 'proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'
    58→
    59→  -- Classification
    60→  account_type TEXT, -- 'general', 'channel', 'campaign', 'lite', 'ad_hoc' (set when qualified)
    61→
    62→  -- Value (set when becomes a deal)
    63→  deal_value DECIMAL,
    64→  currency TEXT DEFAULT 'USD',
    65→
    66→  -- Tracking
    67→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    68→
    69→  -- Ownership & Source
    70→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    71→  source TEXT, -- 'referral', 'inbound', 'event', 'cold_outreach'
    72→  referrer TEXT, -- Who referred them
    73→
    74→  -- Communication
    75→  gc TEXT, -- Group Chat link/info
    76→
    77→  -- Affiliate connection (for commission tracking)
    78→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
    79→
    80→  -- Notes
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create storage bucket for form attachments
     2→-- This allows public form submissions to upload files
     3→
     4→-- Create the storage bucket
     5→INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
     6→VALUES (
     7→  'form-attachments',
     8→  'form-attachments',
     9→  true, -- Public bucket so anyone can read the files
    10→  10485760, -- 10MB file size limit
    11→  ARRAY[
    12→    'image/jpeg',
    13→    'image/png',
    14→    'image/gif',
    15→    'image/webp',
    16→    'application/pdf',
    17→    'application/msword',
    18→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
    19→    'application/vnd.ms-excel',
    20→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
    21→    'text/plain',
    22→    'text/csv'
    23→  ]
    24→)
    25→ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    26→
    27→-- Create storage policies
    28→
    29→-- Allow anyone to upload files (for public form submissions)
    30→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments"
    31→  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
    32→  WITH CHECK (
    33→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    34→  );
    35→
    36→-- Allow anyone to read files (public bucket)
    37→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments"
    38→  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
    39→  USING (
    40→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    41→  );
    42→
    43→-- Allow form owners and admins to delete attachments
    44→CREATE POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments"
    45→  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
    46→  USING (
    47→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    48→    AND (
    49→      -- Check if user owns the form (path format: formId/fieldId/filename)
    50→      EXISTS (
    51→        SELECT 1 FROM forms
    52→        WHERE forms.id::text = split_part(name, '/', 1)
    53→        AND forms.user_id = auth.uid()
    54→      )
    55→      OR
    56→      -- Check if user is admin
    57→      EXISTS (
    58→        SELECT 1 FROM users
    59→        WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    60→        AND users.role = 'admin'
    61→      )
    62→    )
    63→  );
    64→
    65→-- Add comment for documentation
    66→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    67→  'Allows anyone (including anonymous users) to upload attachments when submitting forms';
    68→
    69→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    70→  'Allows public access to read form attachments since the bucket is public';
    71→
    72→COMMENT ON POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    73→  'Allows form owners to delete attachments for their forms, and admins to delete any attachment';
    74→
```
</details></tool-use>
