   572→    if (!addingToGroup || !newRowName.trim()) {
   573→      setAddingToGroup(null);
   574→      setNewRowName('');
   575→      return;
   576→    }
   577→
   578→    const groupKey = addingToGroup;
   579→    const name = newRowName.trim();
   580→    setAddingToGroup(null);
   581→    setNewRowName('');
   582→
   583→    if (!user?.id || !userProfile) return;
   584→
   585→    try {
   586→      const assignedTo = groupKey === '_unassigned' ? null : groupKey;
   587→      const assignedMember = assignedTo ? teamMembers.find(m => m.id === assignedTo) : null;
   588→      const defaultFrequency = activeTab === 'one-time' ? 'one-time' : activeTab === 'recurring' ? 'daily' : 'one-time';
   589→      const defaultType = activeTab === 'client-sop' ? 'Client SOP' : 'General';
   590→
   591→      const { error } = await supabase.from('tasks').insert({
   592→        task_name: name,
   593→        assigned_to: assignedTo,
   594→        assigned_to_name: assignedMember?.name || null,
   595→        frequency: defaultFrequency,
   596→        task_type: defaultType,
   597→        created_by: user.id,
   598→        created_by_name: userProfile.name || userProfile.email || 'Unknown',
   599→      });
   600→      if (error) throw error;
   601→      await fetchTasks();
   602→    } catch (err) {
   603→      console.error('Error adding task:', err);
   604→      toast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Failed to add task.', variant: 'destructive' });
   605→    }
   606→  };
   607→
   608→  const formatDate = (dateStr: string) => {
   609→    return new Date(dateStr + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });
   610→  };
   611→
   612→  const userColors = [
   613→    { bg: 'bg-blue-50', text: 'text-blue-700', border: 'border-blue-200' },
   614→    { bg: 'bg-emerald-50', text: 'text-emerald-700', border: 'border-emerald-200' },
   615→    { bg: 'bg-purple-50', text: 'text-purple-700', border: 'border-purple-200' },
   616→    { bg: 'bg-amber-50', text: 'text-amber-700', border: 'border-amber-200' },
   617→    { bg: 'bg-cyan-50', text: 'text-cyan-700', border: 'border-cyan-200' },
   618→    { bg: 'bg-pink-50', text: 'text-pink-700', border: 'border-pink-200' },
   619→    { bg: 'bg-indigo-50', text: 'text-indigo-700', border: 'border-indigo-200' },
   620→    { bg: 'bg-orange-50', text: 'text-orange-700', border: 'border-orange-200' },
   621→    { bg: 'bg-teal-50', text: 'text-teal-700', border: 'border-teal-200' },
   622→    { bg: 'bg-slate-50', text: 'text-slate-600', border: 'border-slate-200' },
   623→  ];
   624→
   625→  const getColorForIndex = (idx: number) => userColors[idx % userColors.length];
   626→
   627→  const getUserInitials = (name: string) => {
   628→    return name.split(' ').map(w => w[0]).join('').slice(0, 2).toUpperCase();
   629→  };
   630→
   631→  const colLabel: Record<ColumnKey, string> = {
   632→    taskName: 'Task Name', dueDate: 'Due Date', comment: 'Comment',
   633→    frequency: 'Frequency', type: 'Type', link: 'Link',
   634→    createdBy: 'Created By', created: 'Created',
   635→  };
   636→
   637→  const tableHeader = (
   638→    <thead>
   639→      <tr className="border-b border-gray-200 bg-gray-50/80">
   640→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.reorder}`}></th>
   641→        <th className={`py-3 px-1 ${COL.status}`}></th>
   642→        {columnOrder.map((col) => (
   643→          <th
   644→            key={col}
   645→            className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL[col]} cursor-grab select-none`}
   646→            draggable
   647→            onDragStart={() => handleColumnDragStart(col)}
   648→            onDragOver={(e) => handleColumnDragOver(e, col)}
   649→            onDrop={handleColumnDrop}
   650→          >
   651→            <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
   652→              <GripVertical className="h-3 w-3 text-gray-300 flex-shrink-0" />
   653→              {colLabel[col]}
   654→            </div>
   655→          </th>
   656→        ))}
   657→        <th className={`text-right py-3 px-3 ${COL.actions}`}></th>
   658→      </tr>
   659→    </thead>
   660→  );
   661→
   662→  const renderCell = (task: Task, col: ColumnKey) => {
   663→    const isEditingField = (field: string) => editingCell?.taskId === task.id && editingCell?.field === field;
   664→
   665→    switch (col) {
   666→      case 'taskName':
   667→        return (
   668→          <td key={col} className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.taskName}`}>
   669→            {isEditingField('task_name') ? (
   670→              <Input
   671→                value={editingValue}
   672→                onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   673→                onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   674→                onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') saveInlineEdit(); if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   675→                className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-sm font-medium text-gray-900"
   676→                style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   677→                autoFocus
   678→              />
   679→            ) : task.description && task.description !== '<p><br></p>' ? (
   680→              <TooltipProvider delayDuration={200}>
   681→                <Tooltip>
   682→                  <TooltipTrigger asChild>
   683→                    <span
   684→                      className="text-gray-900 font-medium line-clamp-2 cursor-pointer"
   685→                      onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'task_name', task.task_name)}
   686→                    >
   687→                      {task.task_name}
   688→                    </span>
   689→                  </TooltipTrigger>
   690→                  <TooltipContent side="bottom" align="start" className="max-w-xs text-xs">
   691→                    <div className="max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto">
   692→                      <div className="prose prose-xs max-w-none [&_p]:m-0 [&_ul]:m-0 [&_ol]:m-0 [&_li]:m-0" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: task.description }} />
   693→                    </div>
   694→                  </TooltipContent>
   695→                </Tooltip>
   696→              </TooltipProvider>
   697→            ) : (
   698→              <span
   699→                className="text-gray-900 font-medium line-clamp-2 cursor-pointer"
   700→                onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'task_name', task.task_name)}
   701→                title="Double-click to edit"
   702→              >
   703→                {task.task_name}
   704→              </span>
   705→            )}
   706→          </td>
   707→        );
   708→      case 'dueDate':
   709→        return (
   710→          <td key={col} className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.dueDate}`}>
   711→            <Popover>
   712→              <PopoverTrigger asChild>
   713→                <button className={`text-sm hover:text-gray-700 cursor-pointer ${getDueDateColor(task.due_date) || 'text-gray-500'}`}>
   714→                  {task.due_date ? new Date(task.due_date + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }) : '—'}
   715→                </button>
   716→              </PopoverTrigger>
   717→              <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-0" align="start">
   718→                <Calendar
   719→                  mode="single"
   720→                  selected={task.due_date ? new Date(task.due_date + 'T00:00:00') : undefined}
   721→                  onSelect={(date) => saveDateField(task.id, date)}
   722→                  initialFocus
   723→                  classNames={{ day_selected: 'text-white hover:text-white focus:text-white' }}
   724→                  modifiersStyles={{ selected: { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } }}
   725→                />
   726→              </PopoverContent>
   727→            </Popover>
   728→          </td>
   729→        );
   730→      case 'comment':
   731→        return (
   732→          <td key={col} className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.comment}`}>
   733→            {isEditingField('latest_comment') ? (
   734→              <textarea
   735→                value={editingValue}
   736→                onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   737→                onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   738→                onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   739→                className="w-full min-w-[140px] border-none shadow-none p-0 bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 text-xs resize-none"
   740→                style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   741→                rows={2}
   742→                autoFocus
   743→              />
   744→            ) : (
   745→              <span
   746→                className="text-gray-600 line-clamp-2 whitespace-pre-wrap cursor-pointer text-xs"
   747→                onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'latest_comment', task.latest_comment || '')}
   748→                title="Double-click to edit"
   749→              >
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { supabase } from './supabase'
     2→import { Database } from './database.types'
     3→
     4→type UserProfile = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']
     5→type UserInsert = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Insert']
     6→type UserUpdate = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Update']
     7→
     8→export class UserService {
     9→  /**
    10→   * Create a new user profile (for OAuth users)
    11→   */
    12→  static async createUserProfile(userData: UserInsert): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    13→    try {
    14→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    15→        .from('users')
    16→        .insert(userData)
    17→        .select()
    18→        .single()
    19→
    20→      if (error) {
    21→        console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    22→        return null
    23→      }
    24→
    25→      return data
    26→    } catch (error) {
    27→      console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    28→      return null
    29→    }
    30→  }
    31→
    32→  /**
    33→   * Get or create user profile (useful for OAuth flows)
    34→   */
    35→  static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
    36→    userId: string,
    37→    email: string,
    38→    name?: string
    39→  ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    40→    // First try to get existing profile
    41→    const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
    42→    if (existingProfile) {
    43→      return existingProfile
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    // Create new profile for OAuth user — inactive until admin approves
    47→    return this.createUserProfile({
    48→      id: userId,
    49→      email,
    50→      name: name || email.split('@')[0],

   963→                  onKeyDown={(e) => {
   964→                    if (e.key === 'Enter') handleSaveNewRow();
   965→                    if (e.key === 'Escape') { setAddingToGroup(null); setNewRowName(''); }
   966→                  }}
   967→                  placeholder="Enter task name..."
   968→                  className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-sm font-medium"
   969→                  style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   970→                  autoFocus
   971→                />
   972→              </td>
   973→            </tr>
   974→          )}
   975→        </tbody>
   976→      </table>
   977→    );
   978→  };
   979→
   980→  return (
   981→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   982→      <div className="w-full">
   983→        <div className="space-y-4">
   984→          {/* Header */}
   985→          <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm p-6">
   986→            <div className="pb-5 border-b border-gray-100 flex flex-row items-center justify-between">
   987→              <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   988→                <div className="bg-gray-100 p-2 rounded-lg">
   989→                  <ListTodo className="h-6 w-6 text-gray-600" />
   990→                </div>
   991→                <div>
   992→                  <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Tasks</h2>
   993→                  <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">Manage team tasks, SOPs, and recurring work</p>
   994→                </div>
   995→              </div>
   996→              <Button
   997→                className="hover:opacity-90"
   998→                style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   999→                onClick={() => openForm()}
  1000→              >
  1001→                <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
  1002→                Add Task
  1003→              </Button>
  1004→            </div>
  1005→
  1006→            {/* Tabs */}
  1007→            <div className="pt-4">
  1008→              {loading ? (
  1009→                <div className="flex gap-2">
  1010→                  {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-9 w-28 rounded" />)}
  1011→                </div>
  1012→              ) : (
  1013→                <Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={setActiveTab}>
  1014→                  <TabsList className="bg-gray-100 p-1 h-auto flex-wrap">
  1015→                    <TabsTrigger
  1016→                      value="one-time"
  1017→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1018→                    >
  1019→                      One-Time
  1020→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{oneTimeCount}</span>
  1021→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1022→                    <TabsTrigger
  1023→                      value="recurring"
  1024→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1025→                    >
  1026→                      Recurring
  1027→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{recurringCount}</span>
  1028→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1029→                    <TabsTrigger
  1030→                      value="client-sop"
  1031→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1032→                    >
  1033→                      Client SOP
  1034→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{clientSopCount}</span>
  1035→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1036→                  </TabsList>
  1037→                </Tabs>
  1038→              )}
  1039→            </div>
  1040→
  1041→            {/* Search */}
  1042→            <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3 pt-4">
  1043→              <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
  1044→                <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
  1045→                <Input
  1046→                  placeholder="Search tasks, assignees, comments..."
  1047→                  className="pl-10 auth-input"
  1048→                  value={searchTerm}
  1049→                  onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
  1050→                />
  1051→              </div>
  1052→              {searchTerm && (
  1053→                <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={() => setSearchTerm('')}>
  1054→                  Clear
  1055→                </Button>
  1056→              )}
  1057→            </div>
  1058→          </div>
  1059→
  1060→          {/* Grouped Tables */}
  1061→          {loading ? (
  1062→            <div className="p-6 space-y-3">
  1063→              {[1, 2, 3, 4].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-12 w-full rounded" />)}
  1064→            </div>
  1065→          ) : filtered.length === 0 ? (
  1066→            <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm">
  1067→              <div className="text-center py-16">
  1068→                <ClipboardList className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-300 mx-auto mb-3" />
  1069→                <p className="text-gray-500 font-medium">
  1070→                  {tasks.length === 0 ? 'No tasks yet.' : 'No tasks match your filters.'}
  1071→                </p>
  1072→                {tasks.length === 0 && (
  1073→                  <Button
  1074→                    className="mt-4 hover:opacity-90"
  1075→                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
  1076→                    onClick={() => openForm()}
  1077→                  >
  1078→                    <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" /> Create Your First Task
  1079→                  </Button>
  1080→                )}
  1081→              </div>
  1082→            </div>
  1083→          ) : (
  1084→            <div className="space-y-4">
  1085→              {groupedByUser.map((group, groupIdx) => {
  1086→                const isCollapsed = collapsedUsers.has(group.key);
  1087→                const colors = group.key === '_unassigned'
  1088→                  ? { bg: 'bg-slate-50', text: 'text-slate-600', border: 'border-slate-200' }
  1089→                  : getColorForIndex(groupIdx);
  1090→                const photoUrl = group.key !== '_unassigned' ? userPhotoMap[group.key] : null;
  1091→
  1092→                return (
  1093→                  <div key={group.key}>
  1094→                    {/* Group Header */}
  1095→                    <div
  1096→                      className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 ${colors.bg} ${isCollapsed ? 'rounded-lg' : 'rounded-t-lg'} border ${colors.border} ${isCollapsed ? '' : 'border-b-0'} cursor-pointer select-none transition-all`}
  1097→                      onClick={() => toggleUserCollapse(group.key)}
  1098→                    >
  1099→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
  1100→                        {isCollapsed ? (
  1101→                          <ChevronRight className={`w-4 h-4 ${colors.text}`} />
  1102→                        ) : (
  1103→                          <ChevronDown className={`w-4 h-4 ${colors.text}`} />
  1104→                        )}
  1105→                        {group.key === '_unassigned' ? (
  1106→                          <div className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full bg-slate-200 flex items-center justify-center">
  1107→                            <User className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-slate-500" />
  1108→                          </div>
  1109→                        ) : photoUrl ? (
  1110→                          <div className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full overflow-hidden relative flex-shrink-0">
  1111→                            <img
  1112→                              src={photoUrl}
  1113→                              alt={group.label}
  1114→                              className="w-full h-full object-cover"
  1115→                              onError={(e) => {
  1116→                                const target = e.target as HTMLImageElement;
  1117→                                target.style.display = 'none';
  1118→                                target.nextElementSibling?.classList.remove('hidden');
  1119→                              }}
  1120→                            />
  1121→                            <div className="h-7 w-7 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center absolute top-0 left-0 hidden text-white text-xs font-bold">
  1122→                              {getUserInitials(group.label)}
  1123→                            </div>
  1124→                          </div>
  1125→                        ) : (
  1126→                          <div className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] flex items-center justify-center text-white text-xs font-bold flex-shrink-0">
  1127→                            {getUserInitials(group.label)}
  1128→                          </div>
  1129→                        )}
  1130→                        <h3 className={`font-semibold ${colors.text}`}>{group.label}</h3>
  1131→                        <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium">
  1132→                          {group.tasks.length}
  1133→                        </Badge>
  1134→                      </div>
  1135→                      <Button
  1136→                        variant="ghost"
  1137→                        size="sm"
  1138→                        className={`h-7 px-2 ${colors.text} hover:bg-black/10`}
  1139→                        onClick={(e) => {
  1140→                          e.stopPropagation();
  1141→                          handleAddNewRow(group.key);
  1142→                        }}
  1143→                      >
  1144→                        <Plus className="h-4 w-4" />
  1145→                      </Button>
  1146→                    </div>
  1147→
  1148→                    {/* Group Table */}
  1149→                    {!isCollapsed && (
  1150→                      <div className="bg-white rounded-b-lg border border-gray-200 border-t-0 overflow-hidden">
  1151→                        <div className="overflow-x-auto">
  1152→                          {renderTaskTable(group.tasks, group.key)}
  1153→                        </div>
  1154→                      </div>
  1155→                    )}
  1156→                  </div>
  1157→                );
  1158→              })}
  1159→            </div>
  1160→          )}
  1161→        </div>
  1162→      </div>
  1163→
  1164→      {/* Add/Edit Dialog */}
  1165→      <Dialog open={isFormOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) { setIsFormOpen(false); setEditingId(null); } }}>
  1166→        <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[600px] max-h-[85vh] overflow-hidden">
  1167→          <DialogHeader>
  1168→            <DialogTitle>{editingId ? 'Edit Task' : 'Add Task'}</DialogTitle>
  1169→            <DialogDescription>
  1170→              {editingId ? 'Update the task details below.' : 'Create a new task for the team.'}
  1171→            </DialogDescription>
  1172→          </DialogHeader>
  1173→          <div className="space-y-4 max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto px-1 pb-4">
  1174→            {/* Task Name */}
  1175→            <div className="grid gap-2">
  1176→              <Label>Task Name <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
  1177→              <Input
  1178→                value={form.task_name}
  1179→                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, task_name: e.target.value })}
  1180→                placeholder="Enter task name"
  1181→                className="auth-input"
  1182→              />
  1183→            </div>
  1184→
  1185→            {/* Description */}
  1186→            <div className="grid gap-2">
  1187→              <Label>Description</Label>
  1188→              <div className="task-editor-wrapper">
  1189→                <style jsx global>{`
  1190→                  .task-editor-wrapper {
  1191→                    height: 200px;
  1192→                    min-height: 150px;
  1193→                    max-height: 50vh;
  1194→                    overflow-y: auto;
  1195→                    border: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
  1196→                    border-radius: 0.375rem;
  1197→                    resize: vertical;
  1198→                  }
  1199→                  .task-editor-wrapper .ql-toolbar {
  1200→                    position: sticky;
  1201→                    top: 0;
  1202→                    z-index: 10;
  1203→                    background: white;
  1204→                    border-top: none;
  1205→                    border-left: none;
  1206→                    border-right: none;
  1207→                  }
  1208→                  .task-editor-wrapper .ql-container {
  1209→                    border: none;
  1210→                    min-height: 120px;
  1211→                  }
  1212→                `}</style>
  1213→                <ReactQuill
  1214→                  theme="snow"
  1215→                  value={form.description}
  1216→                  onChange={(value) => setForm({ ...form, description: value })}
  1217→                  modules={{
  1218→                    toolbar: [
  1219→                      [{ 'header': [1, 2, 3, false] }],
  1220→                      ['bold', 'italic', 'underline'],
  1221→                      [{ 'list': 'ordered'}, { 'list': 'bullet' }],
  1222→                      [{ 'indent': '-1'}, { 'indent': '+1' }],
  1223→                      ['link'],
  1224→                      ['clean']
  1225→                    ],
  1226→                  }}
  1227→                  placeholder="Add a description (shown on hover)..."
  1228→                  className="bg-white"
  1229→                />
  1230→              </div>
  1231→            </div>
  1232→
  1233→            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  1234→              {/* Assigned To */}
  1235→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1236→                <Label>Assigned To</Label>
  1237→                <Select value={form.assigned_to} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, assigned_to: v })}>
  1238→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select team member" /></SelectTrigger>
  1239→                  <SelectContent>
  1240→                    {teamMembers.map((m) => <SelectItem key={m.id} value={m.id}>{m.name}</SelectItem>)}
  1241→                  </SelectContent>
  1242→                </Select>
  1243→              </div>
  1244→
  1245→              {/* Due Date */}
  1246→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1247→                <Label>Due Date</Label>
  1248→                <Popover>
  1249→                  <PopoverTrigger asChild>
  1250→                    <Button
  1251→                      variant="outline"
  1252→                      className="auth-input justify-start text-left font-normal"
  1253→                      style={{ borderColor: '#e5e7eb', backgroundColor: 'white', color: form.due_date ? '#111827' : '#9ca3af' }}
  1254→                    >
  1255→                      <CalendarIcon className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  1256→                      {form.due_date ? form.due_date.toLocaleDateString() : 'Select date'}
  1257→                    </Button>
  1258→                  </PopoverTrigger>
  1259→                  <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-0" align="start">
  1260→                    <Calendar
  1261→                      mode="single"
  1262→                      selected={form.due_date}
  1263→                      onSelect={(date) => setForm({ ...form, due_date: date || undefined })}
  1264→                      initialFocus
  1265→                      classNames={{ day_selected: 'text-white hover:text-white focus:text-white' }}
  1266→                      modifiersStyles={{ selected: { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } }}
  1267→                    />
  1268→                  </PopoverContent>
  1269→                </Popover>
  1270→              </div>
  1271→            </div>
  1272→
  1273→            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  1274→              {/* Frequency */}
  1275→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1276→                <Label>Frequency <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
  1277→                <Select value={form.frequency} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, frequency: v })}>
  1278→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select frequency" /></SelectTrigger>
  1279→                  <SelectContent>
  1280→                    {FREQUENCIES.map((f) => <SelectItem key={f} value={f}>{FREQUENCY_LABELS[f]}</SelectItem>)}
  1281→                  </SelectContent>
  1282→                </Select>
  1283→              </div>
  1284→
  1285→              {/* Task Type */}
  1286→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1287→                <Label>Task Type <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
  1288→                <Select value={form.task_type} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, task_type: v })}>
  1289→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select type" /></SelectTrigger>
  1290→                  <SelectContent>
  1291→                    {TASK_TYPES.map((t) => <SelectItem key={t} value={t}>{t}</SelectItem>)}
  1292→                  </SelectContent>
  1293→                </Select>
  1294→              </div>
  1295→            </div>
  1296→
  1297→            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  1298→              {/* Status */}
  1299→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1300→                <Label>Status</Label>
  1301→                <Select value={form.status} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, status: v })}>
  1302→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select status" /></SelectTrigger>
  1303→                  <SelectContent>
  1304→                    {STATUSES.map((s) => {
  1305→                      const cfg = STATUS_CONFIG[s];
  1306→                      const Icon = cfg.icon;
  1307→                      return (
  1308→                        <SelectItem key={s} value={s}>
  1309→                          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1310→                            <Icon className={`h-3.5 w-3.5 ${cfg.color}`} />
  1311→                            {cfg.label}
  1312→                          </div>
  1313→                        </SelectItem>
  1314→                      );
  1315→                    })}
  1316→                  </SelectContent>
  1317→                </Select>
  1318→              </div>
  1319→
  1320→              {/* Link */}
  1321→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1322→                <Label>Link</Label>
  1323→                <Input
  1324→                  value={form.link}
  1325→                  onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, link: e.target.value })}
  1326→                  placeholder="https://..."
  1327→                  className="auth-input"
  1328→                />
  1329→              </div>
  1330→            </div>
  1331→
  1332→            {/* Latest Comment */}
  1333→            <div className="grid gap-2">
  1334→              <Label>Latest Comment</Label>
  1335→              <Textarea
  1336→                value={form.latest_comment}
  1337→                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, latest_comment: e.target.value })}
  1338→                placeholder="Add a comment or note..."
  1339→                className="auth-input"
  1340→                rows={3}
  1341→              />
  1342→            </div>
  1343→          </div>
  1344→          <DialogFooter>
  1345→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => { setIsFormOpen(false); setEditingId(null); }}>Cancel</Button>
  1346→            <Button
  1347→              className="hover:opacity-90"
  1348→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
  1349→              onClick={handleSubmit}
  1350→              disabled={!form.task_name.trim() || !form.frequency || !form.task_type || submitting}
  1351→            >
  1352→              {submitting ? (
  1353→                <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-4 w-4 border-b-2 border-white" />
  1354→              ) : editingId ? 'Save Changes' : 'Add Task'}
  1355→            </Button>
  1356→          </DialogFooter>
  1357→        </DialogContent>
  1358→      </Dialog>
  1359→
  1360→      {/* Delete Confirmation */}
  1361→      <Dialog open={!!deletingId} onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) setDeletingId(null); }}>
  1362→        <DialogContent className="max-w-md">
  1363→          <DialogHeader>
  1364→            <DialogTitle>Delete Task</DialogTitle>
  1365→            <DialogDescription>Are you sure you want to delete this task? This action cannot be undone.</DialogDescription>
  1366→          </DialogHeader>
  1367→          <DialogFooter>
  1368→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setDeletingId(null)}>Cancel</Button>
  1369→            <Button variant="destructive" onClick={() => deletingId && handleDelete(deletingId)}>Delete</Button>
  1370→          </DialogFooter>
  1371→        </DialogContent>
  1372→      </Dialog>
  1373→    </div>
  1374→  );
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     5→
     6→// Operations chat for internal notifications
     7→const OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID = '-1002636253963';
     8→
     9→// Initialize Supabase client
    10→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    11→const supabaseKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    12→const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Send a message to the HH Operations Telegram chat
    16→ * POST /api/telegram/send
    17→ */
    18→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    19→  try {
    20→    const body = await request.json();
    21→    const { message, chatId, threadId, useTerminalChat } = body;
    22→
    23→    if (!message) {
    24→      return NextResponse.json(
    25→        { error: 'Message is required' },
    26→        { status: 400 }
    27→      );
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    31→    if (!botToken) {
    32→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Bot token not configured');
    33→      return NextResponse.json(
    34→        { error: 'Telegram bot not configured' },
    35→        { status: 500 }
    36→      );
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    // Use terminal chat if requested, otherwise use provided chatId or default to operations chat
    40→    const terminalChatId = process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    41→    const targetChatId = useTerminalChat && terminalChatId ? terminalChatId : (chatId || OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID);
    42→
    43→    const messagePayload: any = {
    44→      chat_id: targetChatId,
    45→      text: message,
    46→      parse_mode: 'HTML'
    47→    };
    48→
    49→    // Add thread_id for topic-based messaging
    50→    // Use terminal thread ID if using terminal chat, otherwise use provided threadId
```

Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Here is the detailed implementation plan.

---

# ClickUp to HHP Task Management Migration -- Implementation Plan

## Current State Summary

**Tasks table** (in Supabase, NOT in migration files):
- 16 columns: `id`, `task_name`, `assigned_to`, `assigned_to_name`, `due_date`, `latest_comment`, `frequency`, `task_type`, `link`, `created_by`, `created_by_name`, `sort_order`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `description`, `status`
- Single RLS policy: "Authenticated users have full access to tasks" (permissive, all operations)
- Only index: `tasks_pkey` on `id`
- 4 rows currently in the table

**Tasks page** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`, 1375 lines):
- Monolithic `'use client'` component with all Supabase queries inline
- No service layer -- direct `supabase.from('tasks')` calls throughout
- Type definition is local (lines 46-63), not from `database.types.ts`

**Existing patterns to follow**:
- Service classes: static methods, import `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` (see `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts`, `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts`)
- Migrations: numbered SQL files in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/` (currently up to `025`)
- Storage: buckets exist for `form-attachments`, `crm-attachments`, `client-logos`, `campaign-report-files`
- Telegram: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` with static `sendMessage()`, API route at `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`
- UI: shadcn/ui components, brand color `#3e8692`, React Quill for rich text
- No `vercel.json` exists (no cron jobs configured yet)
- The `client_action_items` table already exists with 80 rows, used in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx` -- this is a separate "action board" per client, not the same as tasks

---

## Milestone 1: Foundation -- Service Layer + Schema Additions

**Goal**: Extract all inline Supabase logic into a service class. Add `client_id`, `parent_task_id`, and `priority` columns to the tasks table. Add missing indexes. Do NOT change any UI behavior yet.

### DB Migration: `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`

```sql
-- Add new columns to tasks table
ALTER TABLE tasks
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parent_task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS recurring_config JSONB,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS template_id UUID;

-- Add indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_assigned_to ON tasks(assigned_to);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_client_id ON tasks(client_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_parent_task_id ON tasks(parent_task_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_frequency ON tasks(frequency);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_created_by ON tasks(created_by);

COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.client_id IS 'Links task to a client for client-scoped views';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.parent_task_id IS 'Self-referential FK for subtasks';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.priority IS 'low, medium, high, urgent';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.completed_at IS 'Timestamp when status changed to complete';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.recurring_config IS 'JSON config for recurring generation: {interval, day_of_week, day_of_month, next_due, end_date}';
COMMENT ON COLUMN tasks.template_id IS 'FK to task_templates if created from a template';
```

### New File: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts`

Create `TaskService` as a static class following the `ClientService`/`CRMService` pattern. Extract every Supabase call from `page.tsx` into this service:

```typescript
export class TaskService {
  // --- Core CRUD ---
  static async getAllTasks(): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTaskById(id: string): Promise<Task | null>
  static async createTask(payload: CreateTaskPayload): Promise<Task>
  static async updateTask(id: string, updates: Partial<Task>): Promise<Task>
  static async deleteTask(id: string): Promise<void>

  // --- Queries ---
  static async getTasksForUser(userId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTasksForClient(clientId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getSubtasks(parentTaskId: string): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getOverdueTasks(): Promise<Task[]>
  static async getTasksDueSoon(days: number): Promise<Task[]>

  // --- Inline edit helpers ---
  static async updateField(id: string, field: string, value: any): Promise<void>
  static async updateSortOrder(id: string, sortOrder: number): Promise<void>
  static async reorderTasks(moves: {id: string, sort_order: number}[]): Promise<void>
}
```

Types should be defined at the top of the service file (exported), matching the existing `Task` type from `page.tsx` but extended with the new columns (`client_id`, `parent_task_id`, `priority`, `completed_at`, `recurring_config`, `template_id`).

- Replace all `supabase.from('tasks')` calls with `TaskService.*` calls
- Import the `Task` type from `@/lib/taskService` instead of defining it locally
- No visible UI changes -- everything should work exactly the same
- The `client_id` field in the form can be added but hidden/optional at this stage

### Sidebar Change: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx`

- No changes in this milestone. Tasks link stays as-is at line 401-412.

## Milestone 2: Comments, Attachments, Client Linking

**Goal**: Replace the single `latest_comment` text field with threaded comments. Add file attachments to tasks. Add client selector to task form.

### DB Migration: `027_task_comments_and_attachments.sql`

```sql
-- Task Comments (threaded)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_comments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  user_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  parent_comment_id UUID REFERENCES task_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_comments_task_id ON task_comments(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_comments_parent ON task_comments(parent_comment_id);

-- RLS for task_comments
ALTER TABLE task_comments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task comments"
  ON task_comments FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert task comments"
  ON task_comments FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own comments"
  ON task_comments FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own comments or admins"
  ON task_comments FOR DELETE USING (
    auth.uid() = user_id OR
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Task Attachments
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_attachments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
  file_size INTEGER,
  mime_type TEXT,
  uploaded_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  uploaded_by_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_attachments_task_id ON task_attachments(task_id);

ALTER TABLE task_attachments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can upload task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = uploaded_by);
CREATE POLICY "Uploaders and admins can delete task attachments"
  ON task_attachments FOR DELETE USING (
    auth.uid() = uploaded_by OR
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
```

### DB Migration: `028_task_attachments_storage.sql`

Follow the pattern from `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`:

```sql
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
VALUES (
  'task-attachments', 'task-attachments', false, 10485760,
  ARRAY['image/jpeg','image/png','image/gif','image/webp',
        'application/pdf','application/msword',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
        'text/plain','text/csv']
) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- Authenticated users can upload
CREATE POLICY "Auth users can upload task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
  );

-- Authenticated users can read
CREATE POLICY "Auth users can read task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT USING (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
  );

-- Uploader or admin can delete
CREATE POLICY "Uploader or admin can delete task attachments"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE USING (
    bucket_id = 'task-attachments' AND (
      auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1]
      OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin','super_admin'))
    )
  );
```

static async addComment(taskId: string, userId: string, userName: string, content: string, parentCommentId?: string): Promise<TaskComment>
static async updateComment(commentId: string, content: string): Promise<void>
static async deleteComment(commentId: string): Promise<void>
static async getCommentCount(taskId: string): Promise<number>

// --- Attachments ---

static async uploadAttachment(taskId: string, file: File, userId: string, userName: string): Promise<TaskAttachment>

## Milestone 3: Subtasks/Checklists + Dashboards

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_checklist_items (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,

  is_done BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,

CREATE INDEX idx_task_checklist_task_id ON task_checklist_items(task_id);

ALTER TABLE task_checklist_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users full access to checklists"
  ON task_checklist_items FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL) WITH CHECK (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
```

static async toggleChecklistItem(itemId: string, isDone: boolean): Promise<void>

  totalOverdue: number;
}

  tasksByClient: { clientId: string; name: string; total: number; overdue: number }[];
  overallOverdue: number;
  overallCompleted: number;
  staleTasks: Task[];      // no update in 7+ days, not complete
}

interface ClientDashboardData {
  client: { id: string; name: string };
  tasks: Task[];
  deliverables: Task[];    // tasks with task_type = 'Client Delivery'
  overdueCount: number;
}

## Milestone 4: Recurring Task Engine + Automations

**Goal**: Make the `frequency` field actually generate new task instances. Add status-triggered automations.

### DB Migration: `030_task_automations.sql`

```sql
-- Task automations / rules
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_automations (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,

  -- Trigger
  trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'status_change', 'due_date_passed', 'task_created', 'stale'
  trigger_config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- e.g. {from_status: 'in_progress', to_status: 'complete'}
  -- e.g. {days_overdue: 1}
  -- e.g. {stale_days: 7}

  -- Action
  action_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'assign', 'change_status', 'send_notification', 'create_task'
  action_config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  -- e.g. {assign_to: 'user_id'}
  -- e.g. {notification_type: 'telegram', message_template: '...'}

  -- Scope
  applies_to_client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),  -- null = all tasks
  applies_to_task_type TEXT,  -- null = all types

  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE task_automations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage task automations"
  ON task_automations FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Automation execution log
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_automation_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  automation_id UUID REFERENCES task_automations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  action_taken TEXT NOT NULL,
  details JSONB,
  executed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_task_automation_logs_task ON task_automation_logs(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_automation_logs_automation ON task_automation_logs(automation_id);

ALTER TABLE task_automation_logs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can view automation logs"
  ON task_automation_logs FOR SELECT USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
CREATE POLICY "System can insert automation logs"
  ON task_automation_logs FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true);
```

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`

This is the recurring task and automation engine, triggered by Vercel Cron (or external scheduler):

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/cron  (called by cron, secured with CRON_SECRET header)
// 1. Find recurring tasks where next_due <= today and status = 'complete'
// 2. Clone them with new due_date, status = 'to_do', update recurring_config.next_due
// 3. Find overdue tasks (due_date < today, status != complete) -> execute automations
// 4. Find stale tasks (updated_at < now - 7 days, status not complete) -> execute automations
// 5. Log all actions to task_automation_logs
```

### New File: `vercel.json` (project root)

```json
{
  "crons": [
    { "path": "/api/tasks/cron", "schedule": "0 1 * * *" }
  ]
}
```

### Service additions to `taskService.ts`

```typescript
// --- Recurring ---
static async getRecurringTasksDue(): Promise<Task[]>
static async generateRecurringInstance(parentTask: Task): Promise<Task>
static async calculateNextDueDate(config: RecurringConfig, currentDue: string): Promise<string>

// --- Automations ---
static async getActiveAutomations(): Promise<TaskAutomation[]>
static async evaluateAutomations(task: Task, event: AutomationTriggerEvent): Promise<void>
static async logAutomationExecution(automationId: string, taskId: string, action: string, details: any): Promise<void>
```

### Modification to `taskService.ts` -- status change hook

When `updateTask` or `updateField` changes `status`:
- If new status is `complete`, set `completed_at = now()`
- If old status was `complete` and new is not, clear `completed_at`
- Call `evaluateAutomations(task, { type: 'status_change', from, to })`

### UI Changes

- **Modify task detail modal**: Add `recurring_config` editor in the form when frequency is not `one-time`:
  - Day of week selector (for weekly)
  - Day of month selector (for monthly)
  - End date (optional)
  - "Auto-generate next task when completed" toggle

- **New file: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/automations/page.tsx`**
  - Admin-only page to manage automation rules
  - List existing rules with toggle active/inactive
  - Create new rule form: trigger type, trigger config, action type, action config
  - View automation execution log

### Dependencies: Milestones 1-3

## Milestone 5: Telegram Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports

**Goal**: Send overdue task notifications via Telegram. Generate weekly summary reports.

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts`

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/alerts  (called by cron, daily at 9 AM)
// 1. Query overdue tasks (due_date < today, status not complete)
// 2. Group by assigned_to
// 3. For each user with overdue tasks, send TG message via TelegramService.sendMessage()
// 4. Format: "You have X overdue tasks: [list with task names and due dates]"
```

### New API Route: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts`

```typescript
// GET /api/tasks/weekly-report  (called by cron, Fridays at 5 PM)
// 1. Query all incomplete tasks
// 2. Query tasks completed this week
// 3. Query stale tasks (no update in 7+ days)
// 4. Format summary: per-member breakdown, overdue counts, stale tasks
// 5. Send to Operations TG chat via /api/telegram/send
```

### Update `vercel.json`

```json
{
  "crons": [
    { "path": "/api/tasks/cron", "schedule": "0 1 * * *" },
    { "path": "/api/tasks/alerts", "schedule": "0 9 * * *" },
    { "path": "/api/tasks/weekly-report", "schedule": "0 17 * * 5" }
  ]
}
```

static async getStaleTasks(staleDays: number): Promise<Task[]>
static async getOverdueTasksByUser(): Promise<Map<string, Task[]>>
```

### 7-Day Stale Rule enforcement

In the daily cron (`/api/tasks/cron`), add stale detection:
- Tasks with `updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'` and `status NOT IN ('complete', 'paused')`
- Auto-change status to `paused` or flag with a `stale` badge
- Send TG notification to the assignee

### UI indicator on task rows

In `page.tsx` (or extracted table component), add a visual "stale" indicator:
- If `updated_at` is older than 7 days and status is not `complete`/`paused`, show a small orange "Stale" badge next to the task name

## Milestone 6: Templates + Form-to-Task Integration

**Goal**: Allow saving tasks as templates for reuse. Connect the existing forms system to auto-create tasks from form submissions.

### DB Migration: `031_task_templates.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_templates (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,

  -- Template fields (pre-filled values for new tasks)
  task_name_template TEXT NOT NULL,
  default_assigned_to UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  default_frequency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'one-time',
  default_task_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'General',
  default_priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  default_client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),
  default_description TEXT,
  due_date_offset_days INTEGER,  -- e.g. 7 = due 7 days from creation
  recurring_config JSONB,

  -- Checklist template
  checklist_items JSONB,  -- [{text: "...", display_order: 0}, ...]

  created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE task_templates ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can view task templates"
  ON task_templates FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage task templates"
  ON task_templates FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );

-- Form-to-task mapping
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS form_task_mappings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  form_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES forms(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  template_id UUID REFERENCES task_templates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,

  -- If no template, direct config:
  task_name_pattern TEXT,  -- Can include {{field_label}} placeholders
  assigned_to UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id),
  task_type TEXT DEFAULT 'General',

  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE form_task_mappings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage form task mappings"
  ON form_task_mappings FOR ALL USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  ) WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role IN ('admin', 'super_admin'))
  );
```

static async getTemplates(): Promise<TaskTemplate[]>
static async getTemplate(id: string): Promise<TaskTemplate | null>
static async createTemplate(data: CreateTemplatePayload): Promise<TaskTemplate>
static async updateTemplate(id: string, data: Partial<TaskTemplate>): Promise<TaskTemplate>
static async deleteTemplate(id: string): Promise<void>
static async createTaskFromTemplate(templateId: string, overrides?: Partial<CreateTaskPayload>): Promise<Task>
static async saveTaskAsTemplate(taskId: string, templateName: string): Promise<TaskTemplate>

## Implementation Order Summary

| Milestone | Scope | New Tables | New Files | Modified Files |
|-----------|-------|------------|-----------|----------------|
| **M1: Foundation** | Service layer, schema additions | 0 new, alter `tasks` | `lib/taskService.ts` | `app/tasks/page.tsx`, migration `026` |
| **M2: Core Features** | Comments, attachments, client linking | `task_comments`, `task_attachments` | `components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx`, `TaskComments.tsx`, `TaskAttachments.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `app/tasks/page.tsx`, migrations `027`, `028` |
| **M3: Dashboards + Subtasks** | My/Admin/Client dashboards, checklists | `task_checklist_items` | `app/tasks/my-dashboard/page.tsx`, `app/tasks/admin/page.tsx`, `app/tasks/client/[clientId]/page.tsx`, `components/tasks/TaskChecklist.tsx`, `components/tasks/SubtaskList.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `components/Sidebar.tsx`, migration `029` |
| **M4: Recurring + Automations** | Auto-generation, status triggers | `task_automations`, `task_automation_logs` | `app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`, `app/tasks/automations/page.tsx`, `vercel.json` | `lib/taskService.ts`, task detail modal, migration `030` |
| **M5: TG Alerts + Reports** | Overdue alerts, weekly summary, stale rule | 0 new | `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts`, `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` | `vercel.json`, `app/tasks/page.tsx` (stale badge) |
| **M6: Templates + Forms** | Task templates, form-to-task | `task_templates`, `form_task_mappings` | `app/tasks/templates/page.tsx` | `lib/taskService.ts`, `app/api/forms/submit/route.ts`, task detail modal, migration `031` |

---

## Key Architectural Decisions

1. **Subtasks vs. Checklists**: Two distinct concepts. Subtasks are full `tasks` rows with `parent_task_id` (assignable, have due dates, statuses). Checklists are lightweight `task_checklist_items` (just text + done boolean). This mirrors how ClickUp and similar tools separate the two.

2. **`latest_comment` backward compatibility**: Keep the `latest_comment` column on the `tasks` table. When a new comment is added via `task_comments`, auto-update `latest_comment` to the most recent comment's text. This preserves the inline table preview without requiring a join on every task list fetch.

3. **Recurring generation via cron, not DB triggers**: Using a Vercel Cron API route rather than PostgreSQL triggers or pg_cron. This keeps logic in TypeScript where it is testable and debuggable, and avoids needing Supabase extensions.

4. **No breaking changes to existing RLS**: The current RLS policy is wide-open for authenticated users. New tables follow a slightly tighter model (comments/attachments have owner-based write policies). The main `tasks` table policy remains unchanged to avoid breaking existing functionality.

5. **`client_action_items` left separate**: The existing `client_action_items` table (80 rows, used in the clients page) serves a different purpose -- it is an action board with court/phase fields specific to client delivery. It should NOT be merged into the tasks system. However, the Client Dashboard in Milestone 3 can optionally show both `client_action_items` and `tasks` filtered by `client_id`.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx` - The 1375-line monolith that must be refactored into service calls and have its modal extracted. Every milestone touches this file.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts` - New file. The central service class that all task CRUD, comments, attachments, dashboards, recurring logic, and template operations flow through.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts` - Reference pattern for how to structure the TaskService (static class, Supabase queries, typed returns).
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql` - Reference pattern for the task-attachments storage bucket migration.
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` - Used directly in Milestone 5 for sending overdue alerts and weekly reports.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 10:18:05Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Should the dashboards (My Dashboard, Admin Overview, Client View) be separate pages with their own routes, or tabs within the existing /tasks page?**

Options:
- **Separate pages** - New routes like /tasks/my-dashboard, /tasks/admin — each has its own sidebar link and dedicated layout
- **Tabs on /tasks page** - Add dashboard tabs alongside the existing One-Time/Recurring/Client SOP tabs — keeps everything in one place

**For recurring tasks, what should happen when a recurring task is marked complete?**

Options:
- **Auto-clone on complete** - When you mark a weekly task done, HHP immediately creates the next instance with the next due date
- **Daily cron generates** - A daily cron job checks recurring tasks and generates next instances overnight — simpler but slight delay
- **Both** - Auto-clone on complete for immediate feedback, plus cron as a safety net to catch anything missed

**Which milestone do you want to start with? I can plan the full roadmap but focus implementation on one at a time.**

Options:
- **M1: Foundation** - Service layer extraction + schema additions (client_id, priority, subtask support). No visible UI changes yet.
- **M2: Comments + Attachments** - Jump straight to the most visible gap — threaded comments and file attachments on tasks
- **M3: Dashboards first** - Build My Dashboard and Admin Overview first since oversight is the top priority


**Answer:** Separate pages, Auto-clone on complete, M1: Foundation
</details></tool-use>

```md
# Plan: ClickUp → HHP Task Management Migration

## Context
Replace ClickUp with HHP as the sole internal task management system. The current `/tasks` page is a lightweight manual system with 16 fields, no service layer, no threaded comments, no file attachments, no recurring engine, and no dashboards. This plan adds all missing features in 6 milestones, starting with M1 (Foundation).

**Key decisions:**
- Dashboards = separate pages (`/tasks/my-dashboard`, `/tasks/admin`, `/tasks/client/[id]`)
- Recurring = auto-clone on complete (immediate), plus cron as safety net
- Start with M1: Foundation (service layer + schema)

---

## Milestone 1: Foundation (START HERE)

**Goal:** Extract inline Supabase calls into a service class. Add new columns to `tasks` table. No visible UI changes.

### 1a. DB Migration: `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`
Apply via Supabase MCP tool:
\```sql
ALTER TABLE tasks
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parent_task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS recurring_config JSONB,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS template_id UUID;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_assigned_to ON tasks(assigned_to);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_client_id ON tasks(client_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_parent_task_id ON tasks(parent_task_id);
\```

### 1b. New file: `lib/taskService.ts`
Static class following `ClientService`/`CRMService` pattern. Export `Task` type extended with new columns. Methods:
- `getAllTasks()`, `getTaskById()`, `createTask()`, `updateTask()`, `deleteTask()`
- `updateField(id, field, value)` — for inline edits
- `reorderTasks(moves: {id, sort_order}[])` — for reorder
- `getTasksForUser(userId)`, `getTasksForClient(clientId)`
- `getSubtasks(parentTaskId)`, `getOverdueTasks()`, `getTasksDueSoon(days)`
- On status change to `complete` → set `completed_at`; on un-complete → clear it

### 1c. Refactor: `app/tasks/page.tsx`
- Import `Task` type from `@/lib/taskService`
- Replace all `supabase.from('tasks')` calls with `TaskService.*` methods
- No UI/behavior changes — everything works identically

### Files touched:
- **New:** `lib/taskService.ts`
- **Modified:** `app/tasks/page.tsx` (refactor only)
- **Migration:** `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`

---

## Milestone 2: Comments, Attachments, Client Linking

### 2a. Migration: `027_task_comments_and_attachments.sql`
- `task_comments` table: id, task_id, user_id, user_name, content, parent_comment_id (for threading), created_at, updated_at
- `task_attachments` table: id, task_id, file_name, file_url, file_size, mime_type, uploaded_by, uploaded_by_name, created_at
- RLS: authenticated read, owner write, admin delete

### 2b. Migration: `028_task_attachments_storage.sql`
- Storage bucket `task-attachments` (private, 10MB limit)
- Pattern from `018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

### 2c. Service additions to `taskService.ts`
- Comment CRUD: `getComments()`, `addComment()`, `updateComment()`, `deleteComment()`
- Attachment CRUD: `getAttachments()`, `uploadAttachment()`, `deleteAttachment()`
- Auto-update `latest_comment` on tasks table when new comment added (backward compat)

### 2d. New UI components
- `components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx` — extracted from page.tsx dialog, adds client_id dropdown, priority selector, comments section, attachments section
- `components/tasks/TaskComments.tsx` — threaded comment display with reply/edit/delete
- `components/tasks/TaskAttachments.tsx` — drag-drop upload, file list with download/delete

### 2e. page.tsx changes
- Add "Client" column to table
- Replace inline dialog with `TaskDetailModal`
- Comment cell shows count badge → click opens detail modal

---

## Milestone 3: Dashboards + Subtasks/Checklists

### 3a. Migration: `029_task_checklist_items.sql`
- `task_checklist_items`: id, task_id, text, is_done, display_order, created_at

### 3b. Service additions
- Checklist CRUD: `getChecklist()`, `addChecklistItem()`, `toggleChecklistItem()`, `deleteChecklistItem()`
- Dashboard queries: `getDashboardStats(userId)`, `getAdminDashboardStats()`, `getClientDashboardData(clientId)`

### 3c. New pages (separate routes)
- `app/tasks/my-dashboard/page.tsx` — My assigned tasks, overdue, due this week, completed this week
- `app/tasks/admin/page.tsx` — Admin/super_admin only. Tasks per member, per client, overdue counts, stale tasks
- `app/tasks/client/[clientId]/page.tsx` — Per-client task view with deliverables

### 3d. New components
- `components/tasks/TaskChecklist.tsx` — inline checklist in detail modal
- `components/tasks/SubtaskList.tsx` — subtask list using parent_task_id

### 3e. Sidebar changes
- Add sub-nav under Tasks: "All Tasks", "My Dashboard", "Admin Overview" (admin only)

---

## Milestone 4: Recurring Engine + Automations

### 4a. Migration: `030_task_automations.sql`
- `task_automations`: trigger_type, trigger_config, action_type, action_config, scope
- `task_automation_logs`: execution audit trail

### 4b. Recurring logic in `taskService.ts`
- On `updateTask`/`updateField` status → `complete`: if task has `recurring_config`, auto-clone with next due date, status `to_do`
- `calculateNextDueDate()` helper for weekly/monthly/daily

### 4c. API route: `app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`
- Daily cron safety net: generate missed recurring instances, evaluate stale rules
- `vercel.json` cron config

### 4d. UI
- Recurring config editor in task detail modal (day of week, day of month, end date)
- `app/tasks/automations/page.tsx` — admin rule management + execution log

---

## Milestone 5: TG Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports

### 5a. API routes
- `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts` — daily 9 AM: overdue task notifications per user via TG
- `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` — Friday 5 PM: completed/overdue/stale summary to ops chat

### 5b. 7-day stale rule
- Stale badge on task rows (updated_at > 7 days, not complete/paused)
- TG notification to assignee

### 5c. Uses existing `lib/telegramService.ts` for sending

---

## Milestone 6: Templates + Form→Task

### 6a. Migration: `031_task_templates.sql`
- `task_templates`: reusable task presets with checklist items
- `form_task_mappings`: link forms to auto-create tasks on submission

### 6b. Service additions
- Template CRUD, `createTaskFromTemplate()`, `saveTaskAsTemplate()`
- `createTaskFromFormResponse()` with field placeholder substitution

### 6c. UI
- `app/tasks/templates/page.tsx` — manage templates
- "Save as Template" button in task detail modal
- Form builder: "Auto-create task on submission" toggle

---

## Verification (per milestone)
- M1: All existing task CRUD works identically. Build passes. New columns visible in Supabase.
- M2: Create comment → appears threaded. Upload file → appears in attachments. Client dropdown works.
- M3: My Dashboard shows assigned tasks. Admin Dashboard shows per-member stats. Client view filters correctly.
- M4: Complete a weekly recurring task → new instance auto-created with next due date.
- M5: Overdue task → TG bot message received. Friday report sent to ops chat.
- M6: Create template → create task from it with prefilled fields. Form submission → task auto-created.
